# Meridian Airlines — Airport Parking Guide
**Policy Reference:** MA-AP-003
**Effective Date:** 1 November 2025
**Applies To:** Passengers driving to/from Meridian hub airports

---

## 1. Overview

Meridian Airlines has partnered with airport parking operators at its three hub airports to offer discounted parking rates for Meridian passengers.

Parking is not operated by Meridian Airlines. Discounts are facilitated through the Meridian Parking Partner Programme. Meridian Airlines accepts no liability for vehicle damage, theft, or parking disputes.

---

## 2. Dubai (DXB) — Parking Partners

| Facility | Type | Distance | Standard Rate | Meridian Discount Rate |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| DXB Terminal 3 Short Stay | Multi-storey | Attached | AED 20/hour | AED 15/hour (25% off) |
| DXB Long Stay Park 1 | Open lot | 10 min shuttle | AED 50/day | AED 38/day (25% off) |
| DXB Long Stay Park 2 | Covered | 15 min shuttle | AED 70/day | AED 50/day (28% off) |
| Meridian VIP Parking | Covered reserved | 5 min shuttle | AED 120/day | AED 90/day (Gold/Platinum only) |

How to access discount: enter promo code **MA-PARK-DXB** at DXB parking portal (parking.dubaiairports.ae) or present Meridian boarding pass at the payment machine.

---

## 3. London (LHR) — Parking Partners

| Facility | Type | Distance | Standard Rate | Meridian Discount Rate |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| LHR T4 Short Stay | Multi-storey | Attached | GBP 8/hour | GBP 6/hour |
| LHR Long Stay South | Open lot | 15 min bus | GBP 20/day | GBP 15/day |
| ParkMeridian LHR (partner) | Covered | 10 min shuttle | GBP 28/day | GBP 20/day (Gold/Platinum: GBP 16) |

How to access discount: book in advance at meridianair.com/parking/lhr and enter booking PNR (e.g., MA-XKPL92) for automated discount application. Code: **MA-PARK-LHR**.

---

## 4. New York (JFK) — Parking Partners

| Facility | Type | Distance | Standard Rate | Meridian Discount Rate |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| JFK Terminal 8 Garage | Multi-level | Attached | USD 40/day | USD 30/day |
| JFK Economy Lot | Open | 20 min AirTrain | USD 18/day | USD 14/day |
| SkyPark JFK (partner) | Covered valet | 15 min shuttle | USD 55/day | USD 42/day |

How to access discount: reserve online at meridianair.com/parking/jfk. Code: **MA-PARK-JFK**.

---

## 5. Meridian Miles — Miles for Parking

Meridian Miles members earn **1 Meridian Mile per USD 2 spent** at Meridian Parking Partner facilities. Provide Meridian Miles account number (e.g., MA-88234410) at time of parking payment.

Retroactive miles claims for parking: submit within **30 days** of parking date at meridianair.com/missingmiles.

---

## 6. Gold and Platinum Parking Benefits

| Tier | Additional Benefit |
|---|---|
| Gold | Extra 5% discount on top of standard Meridian rate at all partner facilities |
| Platinum | Access to Meridian VIP Parking at DXB; preferred spots at LHR and JFK partner lots |

Gold and Platinum benefits require Meridian Miles card or account number at time of booking.

---

## 7. Booking in Advance

Pre-booking parking is strongly recommended, especially during peak periods (school holidays, public holidays). Discounted rates may not be available for walk-in bookings. Book at:
- meridianair.com/parking
- Meridian app → My Trip → Add Parking

Parking booking reference format: **MA-PKG-XXXXXX** (e.g., MA-PKG-XKP192).

# Meridian Airlines — Airport Transfer Services Guide
**Policy Reference:** MA-AP-002
**Effective Date:** 1 November 2025
**Applies To:** All passengers at Meridian hub airports (DXB, LHR, JFK)

---

## 1. First Class Chauffeur Service

First class passengers are entitled to a complimentary chauffeur service to/from Meridian hub airports.

### 1.1 Coverage
- Available at: Dubai (DXB), London (LHR), New York (JFK)
- Radius: within **75 km** of the airport
- Vehicle: executive sedan or SUV
- Available for arrival and departure

### 1.2 How to Book
- **Online:** meridianair.com → Manage Booking → Enter PNR (e.g., MA-XKPL92) → Ground Transport
- **Contact Centre:** +1-800-MERIDIAN (at least **48 hours** before travel)
- **Email:** chauffeur@meridianair.com

Chauffeur Reference format: **MA-CH-XXXXXX** (e.g., MA-CH-XKP192).

### 1.3 Limitations
- Maximum 1 vehicle per booking (accommodates up to 4 passengers + luggage)
- For larger parties, a second vehicle can be arranged at USD 80 per trip
- Cancellation: free if cancelled 24+ hours before; USD 50 if cancelled within 24 hours

---

## 2. Business Class Airport Bus Service

Business class passengers at DXB and LHR have access to Meridian's dedicated **Business Express Bus** service:
- Runs every 30 minutes between the city centre and the airport
- Price: Complimentary for Business class passengers on day of travel
- Business Express Bus stop: signposted at Arrivals Level, Terminal 3 (DXB) and Terminal 4 (LHR)

---

## 3. Connecting Passenger Transfer Services

Passengers connecting between Meridian Airlines flights on the same PNR (e.g., MA-XKPL92) and with less than **90 minutes** connection time at a hub airport receive:
- Meridian Express Connector service: dedicated Meridian staff escort from arrival gate to departure gate
- Priority security and immigration processing (subject to host country immigration authority approval)
- Automatically triggered when connection is on a single PNR with under 90-minute connection time

For connections with more than 90 minutes, self-connect following standard airport signage.

---

## 4. Minimum Connection Times (MCT)

| Hub Airport | MCT (Domestic–Domestic) | MCT (International–International) | MCT (International–Domestic) |
|---|---|---|---|
| Dubai (DXB) | 45 minutes | 60 minutes | 75 minutes |
| London (LHR) | 60 minutes | 75 minutes | 90 minutes |
| New York (JFK) | 60 minutes | 90 minutes | 90 minutes |

Connections booked on a single Meridian PNR below these MCTs are flagged and may be rescheduled by Meridian. Connections made via separate bookings (self-connect) are the passenger's sole responsibility.

---

## 5. Disrupted Connection Assistance

If a Meridian flight delay causes a passenger to miss their connection on the same PNR:
- Meridian ground staff will meet passengers at the arrival gate with rebooking options
- Next available Meridian or partner airline connection arranged at no charge
- Hotel accommodation provided if no connection available within 4 hours (Business/First) or 6 hours (Economy)
- Meal vouchers provided per MA-DC-001

Contact Meridian ground staff at the Transfer Desk, located at each Meridian hub arrival area. Reference your PNR (e.g., MA-XKPL92) and Disruption Reference if issued (MA-DC-XXXXXX).

# Meridian Airlines — Alliance Benefits Guide
**Policy Reference:** MA-CS-002
**Effective Date:** 1 November 2025
**Applies To:** Meridian Miles members travelling on Meridian alliance partner flights

---

## 1. Meridian's Alliance Membership

Meridian Airlines is a member of the **SkyCircle Alliance**, a global airline alliance comprising 18 member carriers. Alliance membership provides passengers with reciprocal benefits across all member airlines.

SkyCircle Alliance members relevant to Meridian passengers include:
- ArcticAir (AA)
- SunPath Airways (SP)
- Pacific Rim Airlines (PR)
- EuroConnect (EC)
- Southern Cross Air (SC)
- TransAtlantic Express (TE)
- IndoPacific Air (IP)

Full member list: skycirclealliance.com/members

---

## 2. Alliance Lounge Access

| Tier | Alliance Lounge Access |
|---|---|
| Meridian Platinum | Access to any SkyCircle Business or First lounge at any alliance member airport |
| Meridian Gold | Access to SkyCircle Economy Plus lounge (where available) at alliance airports |
| Meridian Silver | No alliance lounge access (Meridian lounges only per MA-AP-001) |
| Meridian Blue | No alliance lounge access |

Access is granted on presentation of the Meridian Miles card or digital card in the Meridian app (account MA-XXXXXXXX) and a same-day boarding pass on a SkyCircle alliance flight.

---

## 3. Alliance Miles Earning

Meridian Miles members earn miles on SkyCircle Alliance partner flights at the following rates:

| Earning Class | Rate |
|---|---|
| Alliance Business / First class | 100% of miles flown |
| Alliance Economy Flex equivalent | 75% of miles flown |
| Alliance Economy Saver equivalent | 50% of miles flown |
| Alliance lowest promotional fares | 25% of miles flown |

Exact earning rates vary by partner airline and fare class combination. Full earning table at meridianair.com/earningpartners.

Always add your Meridian Miles number (MA-XXXXXXXX) to the partner booking before travel to ensure miles are credited.

---

## 4. Through Baggage Check on Alliance Flights

When travelling on a single ticket (issued under a Meridian ticket number MA-XXXXXXXXXXXXX) that includes alliance partner segments:
- Bags are tagged through to the final destination at the first check-in point
- Baggage allowance of the **ticketing carrier's policy** applies (Meridian's if the ticket is MA-issued)
- Bags do not need to be reclaimed and rechecked at alliance partner hubs

For separate tickets (self-connecting), baggage must be rechecked independently.

---

## 5. Alliance Priority Services

Meridian Miles Gold and Platinum members receive the following on all SkyCircle Alliance carrier flights:

| Benefit | Gold | Platinum |
|---|---|---|
| Priority check-in at alliance airports | Yes | Yes |
| Priority boarding | Yes | Yes |
| Extra baggage allowance | +1 bag (varies by partner) | +2 bags (varies by partner) |
| Alliance partner lounge access | Limited | Full |

Partner carriers may have different priority benefit implementations. Meridian cannot guarantee uniform service delivery across all 18 alliance members.

---

## 6. Alliance Award Redemption

Meridian Miles may be redeemed for award flights on SkyCircle Alliance member airlines:
- Miles required are based on the alliance redemption table (typically higher than Meridian own-metal awards)
- Award availability is subject to the partner airline's award inventory
- Taxes and fees are charged in cash at time of booking
- Book via meridianair.com/awardSearch → Toggle "Alliance Partners"

Alliance award booking reference format: **MA-ALA-XXXXXX** (e.g., MA-ALA-XKP192).

# Meridian Airlines — Seat Upgrade Add-Ons Guide
**Policy Reference:** MA-AN-001
**Effective Date:** 1 November 2025
**Applies To:** Economy passengers on all Meridian Airlines routes

---

## 1. Available Seat Add-Ons

Economy Saver and Economy Flex passengers can enhance their seating experience by purchasing the following add-ons. These are seat-category upgrades within the Economy cabin — not cabin class upgrades (see MA-FK-003 for cabin upgrades).

---

## 2. Preferred Seat Add-On

Preferred seats include window and aisle seats and first-10-row Economy seats.

| Route Type | Pre-Purchase Fee | Airport Fee |
|---|---|---|
| Domestic (under 2 hours) | USD 12 per sector | USD 18 per sector |
| Domestic (over 2 hours) | USD 18 per sector | USD 25 per sector |
| International Short-Haul | USD 22 per sector | USD 32 per sector |
| International Long-Haul | USD 30 per sector | USD 45 per sector |

Economy Flex passengers: preferred seats are **free** (see MA-CK-003).

---

## 3. Extra Legroom Seat Add-On

Extra legroom seats offer 91 cm (36 inch) pitch — 13 cm more than standard.

| Route Type | Pre-Purchase Fee | Airport Fee |
|---|---|---|
| Domestic | USD 25–40 per sector | USD 40–55 per sector |
| International Short-Haul | USD 35–55 per sector | USD 55–75 per sector |
| International Long-Haul | USD 50–75 per sector | USD 75–100 per sector |

Fee varies by specific row. Rows closer to the exit have higher fees. Price displayed in Manage Booking → Seat Selection.

---

## 4. Exit Row Seat Add-On

Exit row seats offer the maximum legroom (107 cm / 42 inch pitch on most aircraft).

| Route Type | Pre-Purchase Fee | Airport Fee |
|---|---|---|
| Domestic | USD 30–50 per sector | USD 50–65 per sector |
| International Short-Haul | USD 45–65 per sector | USD 65–85 per sector |
| International Long-Haul | USD 60–85 per sector | USD 85–110 per sector |

**Eligibility Restrictions for Exit Row:**
- Minimum age: 15 years
- Must be physically capable of operating the emergency exit
- Must be willing to assist in an emergency
- Passengers with mobility impairments, who are pregnant, or travelling with infants are not permitted in exit rows

---

## 5. Economy Comfort Bundle

The Economy Comfort Bundle combines multiple seat add-ons with other amenities at a discount:

| Bundle | Includes | Domestic | International Long-Haul |
|---|---|---|---|
| Comfort Lite | Preferred seat + priority boarding | USD 30 | USD 55 |
| Comfort Plus | Extra legroom seat + priority boarding + 1 extra carry-on kg | USD 55 | USD 95 |
| Comfort Max | Exit row seat + priority boarding + extra bag + lounge pass | USD 100 | USD 175 |

Bundles must be purchased at least **6 hours** before departure. Bundle add-on reference format: **MA-EC-XXXXXX** (e.g., MA-EC-XKP192).

---

## 6. How to Purchase

- **Meridian Website:** Manage Booking → Enter PNR (e.g., MA-XKPL92) → Seat Selection / Add-Ons
- **Meridian App:** My Bookings → Select flight → Upgrade Seat
- **Contact Centre:** +1-800-MERIDIAN (USD 10 service fee applies)
- **Airport Kiosk:** 10% discount vs counter price
- **Airport Counter:** Full price

All seat add-on fees are **non-refundable** unless Meridian Airlines changes the aircraft type and the selected seat type is no longer available, in which case a full seat fee refund is issued.

# Meridian Airlines — Boarding Procedures Guide
**Policy Reference:** MA-CK-002
**Effective Date:** 1 November 2025
**Applies To:** All fare classes, all routes

---

## 1. Gate Arrival Times

Passengers must be at the departure gate no later than:

| Route Type | Gate Arrival Deadline |
|---|---|
| Domestic | 20 minutes before departure |
| International Short-Haul | 30 minutes before departure |
| International Long-Haul | 40 minutes before departure |

Meridian Airlines will not reopen the boarding gate after it has closed. Passengers who miss boarding are classified as no-shows (MA-CX-004).

---

## 2. Gate Close Times

| Route Type | Gate Closes Before Departure |
|---|---|
| Domestic | 15 minutes |
| International Short-Haul | 20 minutes |
| International Long-Haul | 25 minutes |

---

## 3. Boarding Zones

Meridian Airlines uses a zone-based boarding system to reduce congestion. Zones are printed on the boarding pass.

| Zone | Passengers | Boarding Order |
|---|---|---|
| Zone 1 | First class | 1st |
| Zone 2 | Business class | 2nd |
| Zone 3 | Meridian Miles Platinum members | 3rd |
| Zone 4 | Meridian Miles Gold members + passengers with infants + special assistance | 4th |
| Zone 5 | Meridian Miles Silver members + Economy Flex (window seats) | 5th |
| Zone 6 | Economy Flex (middle and aisle seats) + Economy Saver (window seats) | 6th |
| Zone 7 | Economy Saver (middle and aisle seats) | Last |

---

## 4. Priority Boarding

The following passengers board in Zone 1–4 regardless of fare class:
- Passengers requiring special assistance (wheelchair, mobility impairment)
- Unaccompanied minors (accompanied to gate by Meridian staff)
- Passengers travelling with infants (under 2 years)
- Active military personnel in uniform

Priority boarding can also be purchased as an add-on for USD 12–25 (see MA-AN-004).

---

## 5. Boarding Pass Requirements

The boarding pass must display:
- Passenger full name
- Flight number (e.g., MA-204, MA-011)
- Origin and destination airport codes
- Departure date and time
- Seat number (e.g., 14C, 32A)
- Boarding zone
- Barcode or QR code for gate scan

Digital boarding passes (Meridian app or phone wallet) are accepted at all Meridian-operated gates. Some airports require a printed boarding pass — this will be noted in the check-in confirmation email.

---

## 6. Document Check at the Gate

At international departure gates, Meridian gate agents perform a final document check:
- Passport validity confirmed
- Visa or entry document check (where applicable)
- Travel health documentation (where mandated by destination country)

Passengers who fail the document check at the gate are denied boarding and are subject to no-show penalties per MA-CX-004. Meridian Airlines accepts no liability for denied entry at the destination.

---

## 7. Seat Changes at the Gate

Seat changes at the gate are subject to availability. Economy Saver passengers may request a seat change for free at the gate if seats are available. Upgrades at the gate must be processed through the gate agent and are subject to upgrade fees (MA-FK-003).

---

## 8. Flight Number Reference

All Meridian Airlines-operated flights are identified by the prefix **MA-** followed by 2–4 digits, e.g.:
- MA-204 (Dubai DXB → London LHR)
- MA-011 (New York JFK → Dubai DXB)
- MA-87 (domestic short-haul)

Codeshare flights operated by partner airlines retain the partner airline's flight number prefix.

# Meridian Airlines — Check-in Procedures Guide
**Policy Reference:** MA-CK-001
**Effective Date:** 1 November 2025
**Applies To:** All fare classes, all routes

---

## 1. Check-in Channels

### 1.1 Online Check-in
Available at meridianair.com or the Meridian app.

| Route Type | Opens | Closes |
|---|---|---|
| Domestic | 24 hours before departure | 45 minutes before departure |
| International Short-Haul | 48 hours before departure | 60 minutes before departure |
| International Long-Haul | 48 hours before departure | 90 minutes before departure |

Online check-in allows seat selection, boarding pass download, and baggage declaration. Passengers with checked baggage must still drop bags at the airport (see Section 3).

### 1.2 Mobile Check-in
The Meridian app supports mobile boarding passes for domestic and international travel. Download the boarding pass to your phone's wallet (Apple Wallet, Google Wallet).

### 1.3 Airport Kiosk Check-in
Self-service kiosks are available at all Meridian-served airports. Kiosk check-in opens 3 hours before domestic departures and 4 hours before international departures.

### 1.4 Airport Counter Check-in

| Route Type | Counter Opens | Counter Closes |
|---|---|---|
| Domestic | 2.5 hours before departure | 45 minutes before departure |
| International Short-Haul | 3 hours before departure | 60 minutes before departure |
| International Long-Haul | 4 hours before departure | 75 minutes before departure |

Dedicated counters for Business and First class passengers, and Meridian Miles Gold/Platinum members.

---

## 2. Check-in Requirements

All passengers must present at check-in:
- Valid booking confirmation (PNR e.g., MA-XKPL92 or e-ticket MA-XXXXXXXXXXXXX)
- Government-issued photo ID (passport for international travel, national ID or passport for domestic)
- Visa documentation (where applicable — see MA-VI-001)

---

## 3. Baggage Drop

Passengers who checked in online or via the app but have checked baggage must use the **Bag Drop** counter:
- Opens and closes at the same times as the airport counter (Section 1.4)
- Business and First class passengers: dedicated bag drop lane
- Meridian Miles Gold/Platinum: priority bag drop lane

---

## 4. Priority Check-in

The following passengers are entitled to use priority check-in lanes:
- First class passengers
- Business class passengers
- Meridian Miles Gold and Platinum members
- Passengers travelling with infants (under 2 years)
- Passengers requiring special assistance

Priority check-in passengers also receive priority baggage tags (tag format: **MA-PRI-XXXXXXXX**), ensuring their bags are loaded last and offloaded first.

---

## 5. Late Check-in and Missed Check-in

If a passenger misses the check-in deadline:
- **Economy Saver:** No rebooking at original fare; subject to no-show policy (MA-CX-004)
- **Economy Flex:** USD 50 rebooking fee to the next available flight
- **Business:** Rebooking to next available flight at no charge
- **First:** Rebooking to next available flight at no charge

Meridian Airlines does not hold flights for late-arriving passengers.

---

## 6. Automated Passport Control (APC) and Biometric Check-in

At Meridian hub airports (DXB, LHR, JFK), biometric facial recognition check-in is available for enrolled passengers. Enrolment via the Meridian app under Profile → Biometric ID. Biometric check-in bypasses the document verification queue.

# Meridian Airlines — Codeshare Partners Guide
**Policy Reference:** MA-CS-001
**Effective Date:** 1 November 2025
**Applies To:** All passengers on Meridian Airlines codeshare flights

---

## 1. What is a Codeshare Flight?

A codeshare flight is a flight marketed and sold by Meridian Airlines (with a flight number beginning **MA-**) but operated by a partner airline using their own aircraft and crew. For example, flight **MA-450** may be physically operated by partner airline ArcticAir, with the same aircraft, crew, and service standards as ArcticAir's own flights.

Codeshare flights are identified in booking confirmations and boarding passes with the notation: "Operated by [Partner Airline Name]."

---

## 2. Current Codeshare Partners

| Partner Airline | Code | Routes | Type |
|---|---|---|---|
| ArcticAir | AA | DXB–JFK, LHR–JFK | Full codeshare |
| SunPath Airways | SP | DXB–SIN, SIN–SYD | Full codeshare |
| EuroConnect | EC | LHR–FRA, LHR–AMS, LHR–CDG | Full codeshare |
| Pacific Rim Airlines | PR | DXB–NRT, DXB–ICN | Full codeshare |
| Southern Cross Air | SC | SYD–AKL, SYD–SIN | Full codeshare |
| ContinentAir | CA | JFK–YYZ, JFK–MEX | Limited codeshare (select flights) |

Codeshare partnerships are reviewed annually. Current active codeshare flights are listed at meridianair.com/partners/codeshare.

---

## 3. What Changes on a Codeshare Flight

When you book a Meridian-marketed codeshare flight, the following apply:

| Aspect | Applies |
|---|---|
| Meridian check-in and boarding procedures | **No** — use operating carrier's check-in |
| Meridian Miles earning | **Yes** — miles earned per MA-MM-001 on qualifying fares |
| Meridian baggage policy | **Partial** — Meridian's policy applies if ticket is on a single Meridian PNR |
| Meridian lounge access | **Yes** — if entitled (check lounge at operating airline's terminal) |
| Modifications and cancellations | Through Meridian (Contact Centre) or operating carrier |
| Seat selection | Through Meridian website/app where possible; operating carrier's website as fallback |

---

## 4. Baggage on Codeshare Flights

If your journey is on a **single Meridian PNR** (e.g., MA-XKPL92) and includes codeshare segments:
- The baggage allowance of the **Meridian ticket class** applies for the entire journey
- Bags checked at the first departure point are tagged to the final destination
- Excess baggage fees are charged by the operating carrier at the airport for that segment

If your journey involves **separate PNRs** (one Meridian, one partner):
- Each carrier's own baggage policy applies separately
- Bags must be rechecked at the connection point

---

## 5. Check-in for Codeshare Flights

For a Meridian-marketed codeshare flight:
- Check in via **Meridian's website/app** if the codeshare partner supports it (shown at online check-in)
- Otherwise, check in at the **operating carrier's check-in counter** at the airport
- Always present the **Meridian PNR** (e.g., MA-XKPL92) — this is the master booking reference

---

## 6. Service Standards on Codeshare Flights

Meridian Airlines markets codeshare flights but does not control the service on board. Meals, entertainment, and cabin standards are those of the **operating carrier**. Meridian cannot guarantee the same service levels as on Meridian-operated flights.

Complaints about service on codeshare flights should be directed to the operating carrier in the first instance. If unresolved, escalate to feedback@meridianair.com with the MA- PNR and codeshare flight number.

# Meridian Airlines — Corporate Account Guide
**Policy Reference:** MA-CO-001
**Effective Date:** 1 November 2025
**Applies To:** Organisations with a Meridian Airlines corporate travel agreement

---

## 1. What is a Meridian Corporate Account?

A Meridian Corporate Account is a commercial agreement between Meridian Airlines and an organisation that provides:
- Negotiated discount fares on qualifying routes
- Enhanced flexibility on ticket changes and cancellations
- Centralised billing and invoicing
- Dedicated account management

Corporate accounts are available to organisations with a minimum annual Meridian Airlines spend of **USD 50,000**.

---

## 2. Corporate Account Number Format

Upon signing a corporate agreement, the organisation is issued a **Corporate Account Number**:
- Format: **MA-CORP-XXXXXXXX** (e.g., MA-CORP-00128374)
- This number must be entered at booking to access negotiated fares
- Fares booked under the corporate account carry fare codes beginning with **MA-CN**

---

## 3. Benefits of a Meridian Corporate Account

### 3.1 Fare Discounts
| Volume Tier | Discount (Economy) | Discount (Business) |
|---|---|---|
| USD 50K–150K/year | 8% off published fares | 5% off published fares |
| USD 150K–500K/year | 12% off published fares | 8% off published fares |
| Over USD 500K/year | 15–20% off (negotiated) | 12–15% off (negotiated) |

Discounts apply to Economy Flex and Business class only. Economy Saver and First class are excluded from standard corporate discounts.

### 3.2 Enhanced Flexibility
- Free date/time changes on all fare classes (including Economy Saver) for MA-CN fares
- No cancellation fees on any MA-CN fare
- Corporate fares are fully refundable regardless of original fare conditions

### 3.3 Centralised Billing
- Monthly consolidated invoice in PDF and CSV format
- Multiple cost centres supported (each cost centre receives its own sub-account)
- VAT/GST invoices available for applicable jurisdictions
- Online reporting dashboard: meridianair.com/corporate/dashboard

### 3.4 Dedicated Account Manager
Corporate accounts with annual spend over USD 150,000 receive a dedicated Meridian Account Manager:
- Single point of contact for all account queries
- Quarterly business reviews
- Early access to route and fare announcements

---

## 4. How to Apply

Submit a corporate account application to:
- Email: corporate@meridianair.com
- Online: meridianair.com/corporate/apply

Required information:
- Organisation legal name and registration number
- Primary contact name, email, phone
- Estimated annual travel spend (flights only)
- Routes of most frequent travel
- Preferred invoicing and billing arrangements

Processing time: **5–10 business days**. A draft agreement is presented for review before activation.

---

## 5. Booking Under a Corporate Account

- Include the Corporate Account Number (MA-CORP-XXXXXXXX) at the time of booking via the Meridian website, app, or TMC (Travel Management Company)
- Confirm the fare code is **MA-CN** on the booking confirmation
- All bookings under the account are tracked in the corporate dashboard

---

## 6. Unused Ticket Management

Corporate accounts receive access to the **Meridian Ticket Exchange** programme:
- Unused MA-CN tickets (fully unused) can be exchanged for a Meridian travel credit (MA-TC-XXXXXX) valid for 24 months
- Credits are pooled at the account level (not passenger level) — any traveller in the organisation can use them
- No fee for ticket exchange under the corporate account

---

## 7. Contact

Corporate Accounts Team:
- Email: corporate@meridianair.com
- Dedicated line: +1-800-MERIDIAN option 6
- Account Portal: meridianair.com/corporate

# Meridian Airlines — Corporate Fare Benefits Guide
**Policy Reference:** MA-CO-002
**Effective Date:** 1 November 2025
**Applies To:** Organisations with a valid Meridian Corporate Account (MA-CORP-XXXXXXXX)

---

## 1. Corporate Fare Classes

Corporate fares are identified by the **MA-CN** fare code prefix. The following corporate fare classes are available:

| Corporate Fare Class | Based On | Key Benefit |
|---|---|---|
| MA-CN-S (Saver) | Economy Saver | Discounted rate; free changes |
| MA-CN-Y (Flex) | Economy Flex | Discounted rate; free changes; full refund |
| MA-CN-C (Business) | Business | Discounted rate; all Business benefits |
| MA-CN-F (First) | First | Negotiated rates for qualifying accounts |

---

## 2. Ticket Flexibility Under Corporate Fares

Corporate fares override the standard fare class rules for changes and cancellations:

| Rule | Standard Fare | MA-CN Corporate Fare |
|---|---|---|
| Date/time change fee | USD 0–200 (fare-dependent) | USD 0 (free, unlimited) |
| Route change fee | USD 75–150 | USD 0 |
| Cancellation fee | USD 0–200 | USD 0 (fully refundable) |
| Refund type | Credit or cash (fare-dependent) | Always cash to original payment |
| No-show penalty | Up to full fare | USD 0 (full refund) |

---

## 3. Meridian Miles Earning on Corporate Fares

Employees travelling on corporate fares earn Meridian Miles to their personal accounts (MA-XXXXXXXX) at enhanced rates:

| Corporate Fare Class | Miles Earning |
|---|---|
| MA-CN-S | 75% of distance (vs 25% for standard Economy Saver) |
| MA-CN-Y | 125% of distance (vs 100% for standard Economy Flex) |
| MA-CN-C | 175% of distance (vs 150% for standard Business) |
| MA-CN-F | 225% of distance (vs 200% for standard First) |

Enhanced earning applies to the individual traveller's Meridian Miles account. Corporate accounts also earn **Company Miles** (see Section 4).

---

## 4. Company Miles Programme

In addition to individual Meridian Miles, Meridian corporate accounts earn **Company Miles** that belong to the organisation:

# Meridian Airlines — Family Seating Guide
**Policy Reference:** MA-FA-003
**Effective Date:** 1 November 2025
**Applies To:** Families and groups travelling with children under 12

---

## 1. Meridian Family Seating Guarantee

Meridian Airlines guarantees that **children under 12** will be seated **next to at least one accompanying adult** at no extra charge, provided:
- All passengers are on the same PNR (e.g., MA-XKPL92)
- The booking is made at least **48 hours** before departure
- The family is travelling on the same flight

If Meridian cannot seat the child next to an adult despite this guarantee, Meridian will upgrade the affected passengers (child + one adult) to available adjacent seats in a higher-category seat at no charge.

---

## 2. Seat Selection for Families

### 2.1 Recommended Approach
To maximise the chance of sitting together, families should **select seats at the time of booking** or as soon as possible via Manage Booking.

| Fare Class | Seat Selection Timing |
|---|---|
| Economy Saver | Middle seats free at booking; preferred/aisle seats purchased anytime |
| Economy Flex | All standard seats free at booking |
| Business | All Business seats free at booking |
| First | All First suites free at booking |

### 2.2 Best Seats for Families
- **Bulkhead row** (Row 14 on narrow-body, Row 20 on wide-body): extra floor space, bassinet hook points for infants, but no under-seat storage
- **3-seat rows** (aisle + middle + window): ideal for families of 3
- **Window pairs** (2-seat rows on wide-body): ideal for a parent and child, allows child to look out and not disturb others

---

## 3. Bassinet Seat Assignment

Families travelling with infants who have requested a bassinet (MA-BS-XXXXXX) are assigned a **bulkhead seat**. This seat cannot be changed once the bassinet is confirmed as the bassinet is fixed to the bulkhead.

Key points:
- Request bassinet early (see MA-FA-001 Section 4)
- Bulkhead rows are premium seats — they are assigned free of charge to bassinet-requesting passengers
- If no bassinet seat is available, the family may be assigned a standard seat and a bassinet may not be available in-flight

---

## 4. Priority Boarding for Families

Families travelling with children aged **under 5** are included in **Zone 4 boarding** (priority boarding), regardless of fare class. This allows time to get settled before general boarding begins.

To use family priority boarding:
- Present the family's boarding passes at the Zone 4 boarding call
- No additional purchase needed
- Priority boarding extends to the entire family group on the same PNR

---

## 5. Families with Multiple Bookings

If a family is on **two separate PNRs** (e.g., due to a split booking), Meridian cannot guarantee the seating guarantee. Passengers should:
1. Contact the Meridian Contact Centre (+1-800-MERIDIAN) to link the bookings and request adjacent seating
2. Do this as early as possible — at least **72 hours** before departure
3. Seat adjacency subject to availability when bookings are not on the same PNR

---

## 6. Aircraft Configuration Reference

| Aircraft Type | Row Configuration | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Narrow-body (A320 family) | 3-3 (Economy) | Good for families of 3 or 6 |
| Wide-body (A350, B787) | 3-4-3 (Economy) | Centre 4-seat rows ideal for families of 4 |
| Wide-body (B777) | 3-3-3 (Economy) | Window pairs on sides; 3-centre rows for families of 3 |

Aircraft type for your flight is shown at meridianair.com → Manage Booking → Flight Details → Aircraft Type.

## 1. Overview of Meridian Fare Classes

Meridian Airlines offers four primary fare classes across all routes. Each fare class is associated with a specific cabin and a set of inclusions, restrictions, and flexibility rules.

| Economy Saver | Economy | S | Low | Budget travellers, fixed plans |

## 2. Economy Saver (Code: S)

### Inclusions
- 1 personal item (up to 2 kg)
- 1 cabin bag (up to 7 kg)
- 1 checked bag on international routes (23 kg); no free checked bag on domestic
- Standard seat selection (middle seats free; window/aisle: USD 15–35)
- Basic in-flight entertainment (streaming on personal device)
- Complimentary water and non-alcoholic beverages on flights over 2 hours

### Restrictions
- Non-refundable (travel credit only upon cancellation)
- Date/time changes: USD 75–200 fee + fare difference
- Route changes: USD 150 + fare difference
- Not changeable within 48 hours of departure
- No lounge access

### Surcharges
Economy Saver fares on international routes include a fuel surcharge of USD 15–45 depending on route length.

---

## 3. Economy Flex (Code: Y)

### Inclusions
- 1 personal item (up to 3 kg)
- 1 cabin bag (up to 10 kg)
- 1 checked bag on all routes (23 kg); 2 checked bags on long-haul international
- Standard + preferred seat selection included
- Full in-flight entertainment
- Complimentary meals on flights over 3 hours
- Complimentary alcoholic beverages on international flights

### Restrictions
- Partially refundable (cash refund after cancellation fee)
- Free date/time changes up to 2 days before departure
- Route changes: USD 75 + fare difference
- No lounge access (purchasable as add-on)
- Meridian Miles earning: 100% of base miles flown

---

## 4. Business Class (Code: C)

### Inclusions
- 1 personal item (up to 3 kg)
- 2 cabin bags (up to 14 kg total)
- 2 checked bags (up to 32 kg each) on all routes
- Any seat selection included (including lie-flat seat on long-haul)
- Dedicated Business cabin with lie-flat seats on wide-body aircraft
- Full à la carte dining, premium wines and spirits
- Access to Meridian Horizon Lounge at all hub airports (DXB, LHR, JFK)
- Priority check-in, priority security, priority boarding
- Arrival lounge access at DXB, LHR, JFK
- Meridian Miles earning: 150% of base miles flown

### Flexibility
- Free date/time and route changes at any time before departure
- Full cash refund with no cancellation fee (except within 24 hours: USD 75)
- Gold/Platinum members: zero fees at all times

---

## 5. First Class (Code: F)

### Inclusions
- 1 personal item (up to 3 kg)
- 2 cabin bags (up to 16 kg total)
- 3 checked bags (up to 32 kg each) on all routes
- Private suite seating on all long-haul Meridian aircraft
- Personalised pre-flight dining at Meridian First Lounge
- Chauffeur service to/from airport (DXB, LHR, JFK — within 50 km radius)
- Dedicated First Class check-in desk
- Access to Meridian First Lounge (separate from Business lounge)
- Spa facilities available at DXB and LHR First Lounges

## 2. Earning Miles on Meridian Flights

Miles are earned based on the distance flown (great-circle miles) multiplied by a class-of-travel earning multiplier.

### 2.1 Earning Multipliers by Fare Class

| Fare Class | Earning Multiplier | Example: DXB–LHR (3,400 miles) |
|---|---|---|
| Economy Saver | 25% | 850 miles |
| Economy Flex | 100% | 3,400 miles |
| Business | 150% | 5,100 miles |
| First | 200% | 6,800 miles |

### 2.2 Bonus Miles for Elite Members

Elite members earn an additional status bonus on top of the base multiplier:

| Gold | +50% | 150% |
| Platinum | +100% | 200% |

|---|---|

Miles expire if the account has **no earning or redemption activity for 24 consecutive months**. Status miles (used for tier qualification) expire annually at the end of the programme year (31 December).

# Meridian Airlines — Interline Agreements Guide
**Policy Reference:** MA-CS-003
**Effective Date:** 1 November 2025
**Applies To:** Passengers on Meridian Airlines itineraries involving interline partners

---

## 1. What is an Interline Agreement?

An interline agreement allows Meridian Airlines to issue tickets that include flights operated by other airlines on a single ticket, even when no codeshare arrangement exists. The passenger buys one ticket (with one Meridian PNR, e.g., MA-XKPL92) but travels on multiple carriers.

This is distinct from:
- **Codeshare:** Meridian markets a partner's flight under its own flight number (MA-)
- **Interline:** Meridian issues a combined ticket that includes another carrier's flight under that carrier's own flight number

---

## 2. Current Interline Partners

Meridian Airlines maintains active interline agreements with over 60 carriers. Key partners:

| Region | Partner Airlines |
|---|---|

## 3. Single-Ticket Interline Benefits

When your itinerary is on a **single Meridian ticket** (MA-XXXXXXXXXXXXX) including interline segments:

| Benefit | Available |
|---|---|
| Checked baggage through-tagged to final destination | Yes |
| Single check-in at the first departure point | Yes (where permitted by partner) |

| Protected connection (Meridian liable if delay causes missed connection) | Yes |
| Meridian Miles earning on Meridian-operated segments | Yes |
| Meridian Miles earning on interline partner segments | No (no earning on pure interline) |

---

## 4. Baggage on Interline Itineraries

The most important rule for interline travel:

- **Single Meridian ticket:** Meridian's baggage policy applies for the entire journey. Bags are through-tagged at the first check-in point.
- **Separate tickets (self-connect):** Each carrier's individual baggage policy applies. Bags must be collected and rechecked at the connecting airport.

Example: If booking MA-XKPL92 shows flight MA-204 (DXB→LHR, Meridian-operated) connecting to flight NA-101 (LHR→EDI, NordAir-operated) on the same ticket, Meridian's Business class baggage allowance applies throughout both sectors.

---

## 5. Disruption Handling on Interline Itineraries

If a Meridian-operated segment is delayed, causing a missed interline connection **on the same Meridian ticket:**
- Meridian is responsible for rebooking the passenger at no charge, including on the interline partner
- Accommodation and meal entitlements per MA-DC-001 apply
- Contact Meridian ground staff at the Meridian service desk, not the partner airline's desk

If the delay occurs on the **interline partner's segment:**
- The interline partner is responsible for rebooking
- Meridian will assist in coordinating but is not financially liable
- Contact the operating carrier's ground staff or service desk

---

## 6. Limitations

- **No Meridian Miles on interline segments** unless the partner has a separate bilateral miles agreement with Meridian (rare)
- **Seat selection** on interline partner segments: not available via Meridian systems; must be done directly with the operating carrier
- **Lounge access** at interline partner airports: not guaranteed unless the passenger has SkyCircle Alliance status (MA-CS-002)
- **Self-transfer itineraries** (separate tickets): Meridian accepts no liability for missed connections

# Meridian Airlines — Medical Clearance Guide
**Policy Reference:** MA-SS-003
**Effective Date:** 1 November 2025
**Applies To:** Passengers with medical conditions requiring special consideration

---

## 1. When Medical Clearance is Required

Meridian Airlines requires a **Medical Information Form (MEDIF)** for passengers in the following situations:

| Condition | MEDIF Required |
|---|---|
| Recent surgery (within 10 days) | Yes |
| Recent cardiac event (within 6 weeks) | Yes |
| Pregnancy beyond 28 weeks | Yes (see Section 4) |
| Contagious disease | Yes (and may be denied boarding) |
| Stretcher travel | Yes |
| Supplemental oxygen required in-flight | Yes |
| Plaster cast applied within 48 hours | Yes |
| Recent major trauma or fractures | Yes |
| Mental health condition with risk of inflight episode | Yes |

Passengers who are fit to fly but require special seating or assistance (wheelchair, aisle seat) do not require MEDIF — use the Special Assistance service (MA-SS-001).

---

## 2. MEDIF Process

### 2.1 Obtaining the MEDIF Form
The Medical Information Form is available at:
- meridianair.com/medif
- Any Meridian Airport Ticket Office
- Contact: medicalservices@meridianair.com

### 2.2 Completing the MEDIF
The form must be completed by a **licensed medical practitioner** (physician, GP, or specialist). Self-certification is not accepted.

The form covers:
- Diagnosis and current condition
- Fitness to fly statement
- Special requirements in-flight (oxygen, stretcher, medication)
- Medications being carried and in what quantity
- Emergency contact and treating physician details

### 2.3 Submission
- Submit MEDIF to medicalservices@meridianair.com at least **72 hours** before departure
- Reference your booking PNR (e.g., MA-XKPL92) in the subject line
- Meridian's medical team reviews and responds within **48 hours**
- Medical Clearance Reference issued upon approval: **MA-MC-XXXXXX** (e.g., MA-MC-XKP192)

---

## 3. In-Flight Medical Equipment

| Equipment | Availability |
|---|---|
| First aid kit | All Meridian flights |
| Automated External Defibrillator (AED) | All Meridian flights |
| Doctor's kit (prescription medications) | All Meridian flights |
| Supplemental oxygen | Available on request with advance MEDIF approval |
| Stretcher | Wide-body aircraft only; advance booking required |

Passengers requiring supplemental oxygen must declare this in the MEDIF. Personal oxygen concentrators (POCs) are accepted if FAA/EASA-approved; list of approved devices at meridianair.com/oxygen.

---

## 4. Pregnancy Travel Policy

| Gestational Stage | Requirements |
|---|---|
| Up to 28 weeks | No MEDIF required; travel as normal |
| 28–36 weeks (single) | MEDIF required; travel permitted with clearance |
| 28–32 weeks (multiple pregnancy) | MEDIF required; travel permitted with clearance |
| Over 36 weeks (single) | Travel not accepted |
| Over 32 weeks (multiple pregnancy) | Travel not accepted |

A letter from the attending physician confirming gestational age and fitness to fly is required for travel between 28–36 weeks. The letter must be dated within **7 days** of departure.

---

## 5. Medication in Cabin

Passengers may carry prescribed medications in the cabin:
- Must be in original pharmaceutical packaging with pharmacy label showing name and prescription details
- Liquid medications over 100 ml require a doctor's letter and must be declared at security
- Controlled substances (opioids, benzodiazepines) require additional documentation per destination country regulations

Meridian cabin crew can store insulin and temperature-sensitive medications in onboard refrigeration on request.

---

## 6. Onboard Medical Emergency

In the event of a medical emergency in-flight:
- Cabin crew will assess and provide first aid
- A physician onboard may be requested via PA announcement
- Medical radio consultation available with ground-based physicians via the flight deck
- In serious cases, the captain may divert the aircraft to the nearest suitable airport
- Diversion decisions are made solely by the captain in consultation with medical authorities

Meridian Airlines accepts no liability for medical outcomes resulting from conditions that were not disclosed before travel.

# Meridian Airlines — Onboard Medical Emergency Guide
**Policy Reference:** MA-HS-002
**Effective Date:** 1 November 2025
**Applies To:** All Meridian Airlines flights

---

## 1. Meridian Medical Capability

All Meridian Airlines flights are equipped with:

| Equipment | Standard | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| First Aid Kit | All flights | Bandages, antiseptics, basic medications |
| Enhanced First Aid Kit | All international flights | Additional medications, blood glucose meter |
| Emergency Medical Kit (Doctor's Kit) | All flights | Prescription medications, IV supplies, AED leads |
| Automated External Defibrillator (AED) | All flights | Cabin crew trained in AED use |
| Oxygen cylinders | All flights | Fixed and portable units |
| Stretcher | Wide-body international flights | Foldable stretcher in overhead storage |
| Sat-med ground support | All flights | Real-time radio/satellite link to ground physician |

---

## 2. Cabin Crew Medical Training

All Meridian cabin crew hold:
- Valid First Aid and CPR certification (renewed annually)
- AED operation certification
- Training in recognition of common medical emergencies (cardiac arrest, stroke, anaphylaxis, diabetic emergency, seizure, childbirth)

Senior cabin crew (Purser / Cabin Manager) additionally hold:
- Advanced First Aid certification
- In-flight medical emergency management training

---

## 3. What Happens During a Medical Emergency

### Step 1: Crew Assessment
Cabin crew will assess the passenger and administer first aid per their training. They will attempt to identify the nature of the medical emergency.

### Step 2: Medical Announcement
If the situation requires medical expertise, the Purser will make a PA announcement requesting any medical professionals (doctors, nurses, paramedics) onboard to identify themselves. Meridian crew coordinate with any volunteer medical professional.

### Step 3: Ground Medical Consultation
The flight deck contacts **MediLink** (Meridian's contracted ground-based medical advisory service) via satellite communications. MediLink provides real-time guidance to crew and assesses the severity of the situation.

### Step 4: Diversion Decision
The captain, in consultation with MediLink and crew, decides whether to:
- Continue to destination
- Divert to the nearest suitable airport
- Declare a medical emergency and request priority landing

The captain's decision is final and is made solely on safety grounds. Diversion may occur even when the passenger believes they can continue.

---

## 4. Diversion Policy

If an aircraft diverts for a medical emergency:
- All passengers are affected by the diversion
- Meridian will rebook all affected passengers on the next available flights at no charge
- Hotel accommodation provided at Meridian's expense if diversion requires an overnight stay
- The diverted passenger's onward travel is arranged at no charge regardless of fare class
- No cancellation fees apply to any passenger on a diverted flight

---

## 5. Liability

Meridian Airlines provides first aid on a reasonable endeavours basis. Meridian does not warrant the availability of specific medical equipment or the medical expertise of volunteer medical professionals onboard. Meridian Airlines is not liable for medical outcomes resulting from in-flight medical events.

Passengers with known medical conditions are strongly encouraged to obtain appropriate medical insurance before travel and to complete the MEDIF process where required (MA-SS-003).

---

## 6. Post-Flight Medical Claims

If a passenger believes that Meridian's handling of a medical situation was inadequate:
1. Submit a formal complaint to feedback@meridianair.com within **30 days** of the incident
2. Include: flight number (e.g., MA-204), date of travel, PNR (e.g., MA-XKPL92), and a detailed account of the incident
3. Meridian's safety team will investigate within 20 business days
4. For serious incidents, Meridian may be required to report to aviation safety authorities (IATA, CAA, FAA)

# Meridian Airlines — Onboard Safety Rules Guide
**Policy Reference:** MA-HS-001
**Effective Date:** 1 November 2025
**Applies To:** All passengers on all Meridian Airlines flights

---

## 1. Seatbelt Policy

- Seatbelts must be fastened **at all times when seated**, even when the seatbelt sign is off
- Seatbelts must be fastened during: taxi, takeoff, landing, turbulence, and whenever the seatbelt sign is illuminated
- The captain may illuminate the seatbelt sign at any time — compliance is mandatory and enforceable
- Passengers who refuse to comply with seatbelt instructions may be subject to removal from the flight and applicable fines under aviation law

---

## 2. Electronic Devices — PED (Portable Electronic Devices)

### 2.1 Permitted Devices
All personal electronic devices (phones, tablets, laptops, e-readers) are permitted throughout the flight when in **Flight Mode (Airplane Mode)**, including during takeoff and landing.

### 2.2 Mobile Phones and Calls
- Voice calls and mobile data (cellular connection) are **not permitted** during the flight
- Voice over Wi-Fi (VoIP calls via MeridianConnect Wi-Fi) are permitted but discouraged in the cabin out of respect for other passengers
- Calls in the cabin are discouraged; designated areas near lavatory bulkheads may be used

### 2.3 Devices with Cellular Restrictions
- All devices must be in Airplane Mode from aircraft door close to aircraft door open at destination
- Bluetooth: permitted for connecting to headphones, keyboards, and other personal devices

### 2.4 Large Electronic Devices
Laptops and tablets may be used during the entire flight including taxi. They must be stowed in the seat pocket or under the seat for takeoff, landing, and turbulence.

---

## 3. Prohibited Items Onboard

In addition to items prohibited in checked baggage (MA-BG-004), the following are prohibited in the cabin:

- **Smoking:** strictly prohibited on all Meridian Airlines flights, including in lavatories. The lavatory smoke detector is a safety device — tampering with it is a federal offence in many jurisdictions. Penalty: USD 2,000–5,000 fine and possible arrest on landing.
- **Electronic cigarettes and vaping:** prohibited for use in-flight (devices may be carried in cabin but not used)
- **Drones:** not permitted in the cabin or in checked baggage without prior cargo approval
- **Hoverboards / personal electric transporters:** not permitted due to lithium battery fire risk

---

## 4. Oxygen and Pressurisation

All Meridian cabin aircraft maintain cabin pressure equivalent to an altitude of **6,000–8,000 feet**. This is a normal and safe environment for most passengers. Passengers with certain medical conditions should consult a physician before flying (see MA-SS-003).

In the event of rapid decompression, oxygen masks will drop automatically. Passengers must:
- Pull the mask towards you to activate oxygen flow
- Place over nose and mouth
- Breathe normally
- Secure your own mask before assisting others

---

## 5. Safety Briefing

A safety briefing is conducted before every flight by cabin crew. Passengers must:
- Pay attention to the safety briefing
- Read the safety card in the seat pocket
- Identify the nearest emergency exit (it may be behind you)

Passengers who have listened to the safety briefing on a previous flight are still required to pay attention — aircraft configurations differ between aircraft types.

---

## 6. Laser Pointers and Illuminated Devices

Directing a laser pointer or any high-intensity light at any aircraft, including Meridian Airlines aircraft, is illegal in most jurisdictions and may result in arrest and prosecution. Laser pointers are prohibited on Meridian Airlines flights.

---

## 7. Compliance and Authority of Crew

Meridian cabin crew have the authority to issue lawful instructions to all passengers for safety and security reasons. Non-compliance is a criminal offence in most aviation jurisdictions. Passengers who refuse to comply may be:
- Diverted to an alternate airport
- Met by law enforcement on arrival
- Placed on the Meridian no-fly list
- Subject to prosecution

# Meridian Airlines — Passenger Conduct Policy
**Policy Reference:** MA-HS-003
**Effective Date:** 1 November 2025
**Applies To:** All passengers on all Meridian Airlines flights and at Meridian-operated facilities

---

## 1. Expected Standards of Conduct

All passengers travelling on Meridian Airlines are expected to:
- Treat crew and fellow passengers with respect
- Comply promptly with all crew instructions
- Follow safety regulations at all times
- Behave in a manner that does not compromise the safety, security, or comfort of other passengers

---

## 2. Disruptive Behaviour — Definition

Disruptive behaviour includes, but is not limited to:
- Verbal abuse, intimidation, or threats directed at crew or passengers
- Physical assault or threatening physical contact
- Refusal to comply with safety instructions (seatbelt, device usage, smoking prohibition)
- Excessive alcohol consumption causing nuisance
- Sexual harassment or offensive behaviour
- Deliberate damage to aircraft or Meridian property
- Interfering with crew duties
- Attempting to access restricted areas of the aircraft (flight deck, crew rest areas)

---

## 3. Meridian Crew Authority

Meridian cabin crew are empowered under international aviation law (Tokyo Convention, Chicago Convention) to take all reasonable measures to restrain or remove a disruptive passenger. Crew authority to issue lawful instructions supersedes passenger preferences. Non-compliance is a criminal offence in most jurisdictions.

The **Purser / Cabin Manager** is the senior crew authority in the cabin. Escalation goes to the **Captain**, who has final authority over all matters onboard.

---

## 4. Alcohol Policy

### 4.1 Service of Alcohol
- Meridian cabin crew follow a responsible service of alcohol policy
- Crew may decline to serve further alcohol to any passenger who appears intoxicated
- Alcohol is not served to passengers who appear to be under 18 years of age (proof of age may be requested)
- Crew may not be pressured to serve alcohol against their judgment

### 4.2 Passenger-Brought Alcohol
Passengers may not consume alcohol brought onboard. Only alcohol served by Meridian cabin crew is permitted.

### 4.3 Refusal to Board
Meridian ground staff may deny boarding to passengers who appear visibly intoxicated and whose behaviour poses a risk to safety. No refund is issued in such cases; standard no-show policy applies.

---

## 5. Consequences of Disruptive Behaviour

| Severity | Immediate Action | Follow-up |
|---|---|---|
| Minor (verbal rudeness, noise complaint) | Verbal warning from crew; moved to different seat if available | Incident report filed |
| Moderate (persistent non-compliance, verbal aggression) | Formal warning; alcohol service stopped; continued monitoring | Possible ban review |
| Serious (physical threats, assault, safety interference) | Restraint if necessary; diversion to nearest airport; police handoff | Prosecution + permanent ban |

### 5.1 Meridian No-Fly Ban
Passengers found to have committed serious disruptive behaviour may be placed on the Meridian No-Fly List:
- Short-term ban: 12 months for moderate incidents
- Permanent ban: for assault, safety interference, or repeat incidents
- Ban reference format: **MA-NFB-XXXXXX** (e.g., MA-NFB-XKP192)

No-fly ban decisions are made by the Meridian Safety and Security team, not individual crew. Appeals may be submitted to security@meridianair.com within **30 days**.

---

## 6. Incident Reporting

Crew members file an **Air Safety Report (ASR)** for all disruptive passenger incidents. Serious incidents are also reported to:
- The aviation authority of the country of aircraft registration
- The destination country's aviation authority (if diversion occurs)
- Local law enforcement (where applicable)

Passengers who witness disruptive behaviour should report it discreetly to cabin crew using the call button or by writing a note if verbal reporting feels unsafe.

---

## 7. Ground Facility Conduct

The same standards of conduct apply at:
- Meridian check-in and boarding areas
- Meridian lounges (Horizon and First)
- Meridian Airport Ticket Offices

Meridian security staff at these facilities have authority to refuse service or entry to any individual behaving in a manner that breaches this policy.

- All window and aisle seats

- Rows 14–16 on narrow-body aircraft

Meridian Airlines guarantees that children under 12 are seated adjacent to at least one accompanying adult at no extra charge, regardless of fare class. This guarantee applies when booking is made at least **48 hours before departure** on a single PNR.

Groups of 6 or more on the same PNR may request block seating via groupbookings@meridianair.com when the booking is made. Block seating is subject to availability but prioritised for group bookings.

## 1. Requesting Special Assistance

Special assistance must be requested **at least 48 hours before departure** to ensure appropriate arrangements are in place. Requests made within 48 hours will be accommodated on a best-effort basis.

- Contact Centre: +1-800-MERIDIAN option 3 (Special Assistance)

A Special Assistance Reference is issued upon confirmation: **MA-SA-XXXXXX** (e.g., MA-SA-XKP192).

Meridian Airlines uses IATA standard wheelchair codes. When requesting assistance, specify the appropriate category:

| WCHS | Cannot climb stairs but can walk short distances | Wheelchair from check-in to aircraft door; boarding lift if needed |

## 6. Guide Dogs and Assistance Animals

Registered guide dogs and trained assistance animals are accepted in the cabin on all Meridian Airlines flights **free of charge**.

# Meridian Airlines — Transit Visa Rules Guide
**Policy Reference:** MA-VI-002
**Effective Date:** 1 November 2025
**Applies To:** All passengers transiting through Meridian hub airports

---

## 1. Types of Transit

| Transit Type | Definition | Visa Requirement |
|---|---|---|
| Airside transit (TWOV) | Remain in international/sterile zone only; do not clear immigration | Usually no visa required (nationality-dependent) |
| Landside transit | Clear immigration, access public areas, hotel, city | Transit or entry visa required in most cases |
| Same-day connection | Connect within the airport on a single PNR | TWOV rules typically apply |
| Overnight transit | Airport transit requiring an overnight stay | Entry/transit visa usually required |

---

## 2. Dubai (DXB) Transit Rules

Dubai is a major Meridian hub with high transit passenger volumes.

### 2.1 Airside (TWOV) at DXB
- Passengers remaining airside do **not** require a UAE visa for transit, regardless of nationality, provided they do not clear immigration.
- All Meridian connecting flights at DXB are within the same terminal complex (Terminal 3) — airside transit is standard.

### 2.2 Landside Transit at DXB
- Passengers wishing to leave the airport (hotel, sightseeing, or overnight stay) require a UAE transit visa or entry visa.
- Visa on arrival is available for many nationalities at DXB for up to **96 hours**.
- UAE visa-on-arrival nationalities: EU passport holders, US, UK, Australia, Canada, Japan, and 50+ others. Check icp.gov.ae for current eligibility.
- Passengers requiring a UAE transit visa must apply in advance via the UAE GDRFA portal.

### 2.3 DXB Overnight Connections
If a Meridian booking (PNR e.g., MA-XKPL92) has an overnight connection at DXB:
- Meridian provides hotel accommodation at a partner hotel for **Business and First class** passengers (and Gold/Platinum members on any fare) at no charge for connections over 8 hours
- Economy passengers: transit hotel at discounted Meridian rate. Book via meridianair.com/transit-hotel or +1-800-MERIDIAN

---

## 3. London (LHR) Transit Rules

### 3.1 UK Visitor in Transit (DATV)
A **Direct Airside Transit Visa (DATV)** is required for certain nationalities transiting through LHR without a UK visitor visa. Nationalities requiring DATV include nationals of Afghanistan, Albania, Bangladesh, and others — full list: gov.uk/transit-visa.

### 3.2 Landside Transit at LHR
UK Standard Visitor Visa or UK ETA (Electronic Travel Authorisation) required for nationalities who require it to enter the UK. UK ETA is required from November 2025 for all visa-waiver nationals not already holding a BRP or UK visa.

### 3.3 Connecting Across Terminals at LHR
Meridian operates from Terminal 4 (T4). Connections to other airlines at different terminals require clearing UK Border Force, which requires appropriate immigration permission.

---

## 4. New York (JFK) Transit Rules

The United States does **not** have an airside transit arrangement. All passengers arriving at JFK, including those connecting to onward flights, must:
- Clear US Customs and Border Protection (CBP)
- Hold a valid US visa or ESTA (Visa Waiver Programme)
- Collect and recheck baggage

Passengers transiting through JFK must hold the same documentation as passengers entering the US. There are no exceptions. Apply for ESTA at esta.cbp.dhs.gov at least 72 hours before travel.

---

## 5. Transit Hotel Booking References

For Meridian-arranged transit hotel accommodation at DXB:
- Booking reference format: **MA-TH-XXXXXX** (e.g., MA-TH-XKP192)
- Available for connections of 6 hours or more
- Hotel shuttle arranged; Meridian ground staff coordinate with the hotel
- Request at the Meridian Transfer Desk at DXB arrivals or in advance by contacting transit@meridianair.com

# Meridian Airlines — Travel Documents Guide
**Policy Reference:** MA-CK-004
**Effective Date:** 1 November 2025
**Applies To:** All routes, all passengers

---

## 1. Responsibility for Travel Documents

**It is the passenger's sole responsibility** to hold all valid travel documents required for their journey, including visas, health certificates, and any other entry requirements. Meridian Airlines will not carry a passenger who lacks the required documentation and accepts no liability for denied entry at the destination or transit point.

---

## 2. Passport Requirements

### 2.1 International Flights
All passengers on Meridian international flights must hold a valid passport.

| Requirement | Standard Rule |
|---|---|
| Validity | Passport must be valid for at least **6 months beyond** the return travel date |
| Blank pages | At least **2 blank visa pages** required |
| Condition | Passport must be undamaged and machine-readable |

Exceptions: Some countries accept passports valid for 3 months beyond the travel date. Passengers should verify entry requirements for their specific destination.

### 2.2 Domestic Flights
For domestic flights within the same country, a government-issued national ID or driver's licence is accepted in lieu of a passport in most jurisdictions. Check local regulations for country-specific rules.

---

## 3. APIS (Advance Passenger Information System)

Many governments require airlines to submit passenger data before departure. Meridian Airlines collects APIS data during check-in:

- Full name (as per passport)
- Date of birth
- Nationality
- Passport number, issue country, expiry date
- Country of residence
- Travel document type

APIS data collected via the Meridian app or website at check-in. Passengers who do not provide APIS data may be denied boarding.

---

## 4. Visa Requirements

### 4.1 Meridian Disclaimer
Visa requirements change frequently. Meridian Airlines provides guidance as a convenience only. **Passengers must verify current requirements** with the relevant embassy, consulate, or official government immigration website.

### 4.2 Common Visa Categories

| Visa Type | Description |
|---|---|
| Tourist / Visitor Visa | Required for leisure travel to many countries |
| Transit Visa | Required for some passengers transiting through a country, even airside |
| Business Visa | Required for business activities in many jurisdictions |
| Electronic Travel Authorisation (eTA / ESTA / ETA) | Electronic pre-clearance required by US, Canada, Australia, UK |

### 4.3 Passengers Without Required Visa
Passengers denied boarding due to missing or invalid visa documentation are subject to Economy Saver no-show penalties or standard cancellation fees depending on fare class (see MA-CX-001 and MA-CX-002). Meridian Airlines may also be liable to return the passenger to their origin country at its own cost under airline carrier liability regulations.

---

## 5. Travel Health Documents

Some destinations require proof of vaccination or health screening. Current examples include yellow fever vaccination certificates for travel to/from certain African and South American countries.

Meridian Airlines checks health documentation requirements at the time of booking. If a health document requirement exists for a route, passengers are notified in the booking confirmation and at online check-in.

Health document requirements: healthtrav@meridianair.com.

---

## 6. Unaccompanied Minor Travel Documents

Unaccompanied minors (under 18 travelling alone on Meridian) must carry:
- Valid passport (international) or national ID (domestic)
- Unaccompanied Minor form (MA-UM-FORM) completed by the booking guardian
- Emergency contact details for both the sending and receiving adults

See MA-SS-002 for full Unaccompanied Minor policy.

# Meridian Airlines — Travel Health Requirements Guide
**Policy Reference:** MA-VI-003
**Effective Date:** 1 November 2025
**Applies To:** All passengers on Meridian Airlines international flights

---

## 1. Overview

Some countries require proof of specific vaccinations or health documentation as a condition of entry. Meridian Airlines checks health documentation requirements at the time of booking and notifies passengers in the confirmation email.

**Passengers are responsible for obtaining and carrying all required health documents.** Meridian provides this guide for convenience only; requirements may change without notice.

---

## 2. Yellow Fever Vaccination

### 2.1 Who Must Carry a Yellow Fever Certificate
A Yellow Fever Vaccination Certificate (YVFC), also known as the "Yellow Card" or Carte Jaune, is required by certain countries in:
- Sub-Saharan Africa (e.g., Nigeria, Ghana, Kenya, Uganda, Cameroon)
- South America (e.g., Brazil, Colombia, Peru, Ecuador)

Requirements apply in two scenarios:
1. **Entry requirement:** The destination country requires proof of yellow fever vaccination for all arriving passengers
2. **Transit country:** The country requires proof if the passenger has transited through a yellow fever-endemic country in the past 6 days

### 2.2 Certificate Format
- Must be issued by an authorised vaccination centre
- Must include: passenger name, date of birth, vaccine name and batch number, date of vaccination, vaccinating clinician's signature and stamp
- Valid for **life** (per WHO regulations since 2016 — the former 10-year limit no longer applies)

### 2.3 Failure to Present
Passengers unable to present a required YVFC may be vaccinated at the airport (if a vaccination centre is available) or denied entry. Meridian Airlines may deny boarding to passengers without required documentation to avoid fines and return-flight obligations.

---

## 3. Other Required Vaccinations (Destination-Specific)

| Destination Region | Possible Requirements |
|---|---|
| Saudi Arabia (Hajj / Umrah season) | Meningococcal meningitis vaccine certificate required |
| India | Cholera vaccination recommended (not mandatory); yellow fever if transiting endemic country |
| Sub-Saharan Africa (general) | Yellow fever (see above); typhoid and malaria prophylaxis recommended |
| South America | Yellow fever (some countries mandatory); typhoid recommended |

Recommended vaccinations are not checked by Meridian but are strongly advised by the WHO and health authorities. Passengers should consult a travel health clinic at least **4–6 weeks before travel**.

---

## 4. Health Declarations

Some countries periodically require incoming passengers to complete a health declaration form. Forms may be:
- Paper (collected onboard or at immigration)
- Digital (submitted in advance via the destination country's passenger arrival system)

When health declarations are required, Meridian Airlines will:
- Notify passengers at time of booking
- Include declaration form link in the check-in reminder email
- Distribute paper forms during the flight if applicable

---

## 5. Fitness to Fly

Passengers with communicable diseases or symptoms including fever over 38°C, persistent cough, vomiting, or diarrhoea may be denied boarding if considered a health risk to other passengers. Meridian Airlines follows IATA and WHO health risk protocols.

Passengers who are unsure about their fitness to fly should consult a physician before travel. See MA-SS-003 for medical clearance requirements.

---

## 6. Resources for Health Requirements

| Resource | Details |
|---|---|
