# Meridian Airlines — Unaccompanied Minor (UM) Policy
**Policy Reference:** MA-SS-002
**Effective Date:** 1 November 2025
**Applies To:** Children travelling without an accompanying adult

---

## 1. Age Eligibility

| Age | UM Service |
|---|---|
| Under 5 years | Not accepted as unaccompanied minor on any Meridian flight |
| 5–11 years | Mandatory UM service (compulsory, no opt-out) |
| 12–15 years | Optional UM service (parent/guardian may request) |
| 16–17 years | Travel as adult; UM service not available |

---

## 2. UM Service Fee

The Unaccompanied Minor service fee is charged per sector (one way) and is separate from the air ticket fare.

| Route Type | UM Fee per Sector |
|---|---|
| Domestic | USD 60 per sector |
| International Short-Haul | USD 80 per sector |
| International Long-Haul | USD 100 per sector |

The UM fee is non-refundable once the booking is confirmed, unless Meridian Airlines cancels the flight.

---

## 3. Booking a UM

UM travel cannot be booked online. All UM bookings must be made via:
- Meridian Contact Centre: +1-800-MERIDIAN
- Airport Ticket Office

Required at booking:
- Child's full name, date of birth, nationality
- Sending adult's name, relationship, contact phone number
- Receiving adult's name, relationship, contact phone number, address
- Confirmation that the receiving adult will be at the destination airport

UM booking reference format: **MA-UM-XXXXXX** (e.g., MA-UM-XKP192).

---

## 4. Restricted Routes for UMs

Unaccompanied Minors are **not accepted** on:
- Flights with connections where the connection time is less than 2 hours
- Flights arriving after 22:00 local time at the destination (except where a direct supervised handoff is pre-arranged)
- The last flight of the day on routes with no alternative if the flight is cancelled
- Codeshare flights operated by partner airlines (Meridian-operated only)

---

## 5. Airport Handling

### 5.1 Sending Airport
- Sending adult must check the child in at the **UM check-in desk** (not self-service)
- Sending adult must remain at the airport until the flight has departed
- Sending adult must present valid photo ID
- UM is escorted to the gate and onto the aircraft by Meridian ground staff

### 5.2 Onboard
- Flight crew is notified of the UM's presence
- UM is assigned a seat near the front of the cabin (bulkhead where possible)
- Crew monitors the child throughout the flight

### 5.3 Destination Airport
- UM is escorted off the aircraft by Meridian staff
- UM is held in the Meridian UM lounge until the receiving adult arrives
- Receiving adult must present valid photo ID and a copy of the UM booking confirmation (MA-UM-XXXXXX)
- If the receiving adult does not appear within **3 hours**, local child welfare authorities may be contacted

---

## 6. Documents Required

| Document | Required For |
|---|---|
| Completed MA-UM-FORM | All UM journeys |
| Child's passport or ID | Domestic and international |
| Sending adult's photo ID | Sending airport check-in |
| Receiving adult's photo ID | Destination airport collection |

The MA-UM-FORM is available at meridianair.com/umform or at any Meridian Ticket Office.

---

## 7. Connections

UM with connecting Meridian flights on the same PNR (e.g., MA-XKPL92) will be escorted between gates by Meridian staff during the layover. The minimum connection time for UM travel is **2 hours**. UM cannot travel on separate PNRs for connecting segments.

# Meridian Airlines — Visa & Entry Requirements Guide
**Policy Reference:** MA-VI-001
**Effective Date:** 1 November 2025
**Applies To:** All international passengers on Meridian Airlines flights

---

## 1. Passenger Responsibility

**Meridian Airlines does not process visa applications and is not responsible for verifying a passenger's entry eligibility.** The sole responsibility for holding valid and complete travel documentation rests with the passenger.

Meridian Airlines provides general guidance as a convenience. Requirements change frequently and without notice. Passengers must verify requirements with:
- The destination country's official embassy or consulate
- Official government immigration websites
- IATA Travel Centre (timaticweb.com) — the industry standard database

---

## 2. Consequences of Missing Documentation

If a passenger boards a Meridian Airlines flight without required entry documentation:
- The passenger may be denied entry at the destination and returned to the origin
- Meridian Airlines is obligated under ICAO regulations to return the passenger and may be fined by the destination country
- **The passenger bears the full cost of return carriage**
- No refund is issued on the original ticket; no-show/cancellation penalties apply per MA-CX-001/002

---

## 3. Common Electronic Travel Authorisations (ETAs)

Many countries require electronic pre-authorisation before travel, separate from a visa. Key examples relevant to Meridian routes:

| Destination | ETA/ESTA Type | Who Needs It | Application |
|---|---|---|---|
| United States | ESTA | Visa Waiver Programme nationals | esta.cbp.dhs.gov |
| Canada | eTA | Most non-US foreign nationals | canada.ca/eta |
| United Kingdom | ETA | Non-visa nationals from designated countries | gov.uk/guidance/apply-for-an-electronic-travel-authorisation |
| Australia | ETA or eVisitor | Passport-dependent | immi.homeaffairs.gov.au |
| New Zealand | NZeTA | Most visitors | immigration.govt.nz |
| UAE (Dubai hub) | UAE visa on arrival | Many nationalities eligible | Check icp.gov.ae |

Processing times for ETAs/ESTAs range from 15 minutes to several days. Passengers should apply well in advance of travel.

---

## 4. Visas on Arrival

Some destinations served by Meridian Airlines offer visa-on-arrival (VOA) to eligible nationalities. VOA availability depends on:
- Passenger's passport/nationality
- Duration and purpose of stay
- Travel history and current geopolitical conditions

Meridian Airlines cannot guarantee VOA will be available or granted at any destination. Passengers who intend to rely on VOA should have proof of return travel (Meridian booking confirmation with PNR, e.g., MA-XKPL92) and sufficient funds readily available.

---

## 5. Transit Visa Requirements

A transit visa may be required even if a passenger is not entering the country but merely transiting through the airport:

- **Airside transit (TWOV):** Remaining in the international zone without passing through immigration
- **Landside transit:** Clearing immigration to transit (e.g., to access a different terminal)

Transit visa requirements are highly nationality-specific. Reference MA-VI-002 for detailed transit rules by route.

---

## 6. Checking Requirements Before Travel

Meridian Airlines recommends the following verification timeline:

| Timing Before Travel | Action |
|---|---|
| At time of booking | Check IATA Timatic via meridianair.com/travelinfo |
| 4–6 weeks before | Apply for visa if required |
| 1 week before | Recheck requirements for any recent changes |
| Day before | Confirm all documents are in order and easily accessible |

Meridian Travel Information Tool: meridianair.com/travelinfo (enter nationality, destination, transit point). Results are powered by IATA Timatic and are updated in real time.

# Meridian Airlines — General Booking Policy
**Policy Reference:** MA-BK-001
**Effective Date:** 1 November 2025
**Applies To:** All fare classes, all routes, all passengers

---

## 1. Booking Channels

Meridian Airlines tickets may be purchased through the following authorised channels:

- **Meridian Website:** www.meridianair.com (24/7 availability)
- **Meridian Mobile App:** iOS and Android (24/7 availability)
- **Meridian Contact Centre:** Available 24/7 at +1-800-MERIDIAN (+1-800-637-4326)
- **Authorised Travel Agents:** IATA-accredited agents with valid ARC/BSP participation
- **Airport Ticket Offices:** Available during operating hours at all Meridian-served airports

Bookings made through unauthorised third-party resellers are not guaranteed and Meridian Airlines accepts no liability for such transactions.

---

## 2. Booking Window

| Route Type | Minimum Advance | Maximum Advance |
|---|---|---|
| Domestic | 2 hours before departure | 355 days |
| International Short-Haul | 3 hours before departure | 355 days |
| International Long-Haul | 4 hours before departure | 355 days |

Bookings made within 24 hours of departure must be completed via the Contact Centre or Airport Ticket Office.

---

## 3. Payment Rules

### 3.1 Accepted Payment Methods
- Visa, Mastercard, American Express, Discover
- Meridian Wallet (prepaid balance)
- Meridian Miles points (full or partial redemption)
- Bank transfer (corporate accounts only; minimum 72 hours processing)
- Cash (Airport Ticket Offices only)

### 3.2 Payment Deadlines
- **Economy Saver:** Full payment required at time of booking. Seat is not held without payment.
- **Economy Flex:** Full payment required within 24 hours of booking initiation.
- **Business:** Full payment required within 48 hours of booking initiation.
- **First:** Full payment required within 48 hours of booking initiation.
- **Group Bookings (10+ passengers):** 20% deposit at booking; balance due 30 days before departure.

### 3.3 Currency
All fares are displayed and charged in USD unless the booking originates in a country where local currency pricing is mandated by regulation. Currency conversion fees may apply for non-USD cards.

---

## 4. Booking Confirmation

Upon successful payment, a booking confirmation is issued containing:

- **PNR (Passenger Name Record):** A unique 6-character alphanumeric code (e.g., MA-XKPL92) that serves as the booking reference across all Meridian Airlines systems.
- **e-Ticket Number:** A 13-digit numeric code prefixed with MA (e.g., MA-1762345678901) representing the electronic ticket.
- **Itinerary Details:** Flight number, origin, destination, date, time, fare class, seat assignment (if selected).

Confirmation is sent via email within 5 minutes of payment. If not received within 30 minutes, passengers should contact the Contact Centre with their payment reference.

---

## 5. Passenger Name Requirements

Passenger names must exactly match the government-issued travel document to be used for the journey.

- Names must be entered as: **LAST NAME / FIRST NAME MIDDLE NAME**
- Titles (Mr, Mrs, Dr) are optional and do not appear on the boarding pass.
- Suffixes (Jr, Sr) must be included if present on the travel document.
- Spaces, hyphens, and apostrophes in names must be entered as shown on the document.

**Meridian Airlines is not liable for denied boarding due to name discrepancies between the ticket and travel document.**

---

## 6. Itinerary Rules

### 6.1 One-Way and Return Tickets
Both one-way and return tickets are available on all routes. Return tickets on Economy Saver fare may carry restrictions on minimum stay requirements — refer to the fare rules at time of booking.

### 6.2 Open-Jaw Tickets
Open-jaw itineraries (fly into one city, return from another) are permitted on Economy Flex, Business, and First class tickets. Not available on Economy Saver.

### 6.3 Stopover
A stopover of more than 24 hours at an intermediate point is permitted on Business and First class tickets only. Economy fares do not permit stopovers.

---

## 7. Booking Hold (Option)

Meridian Airlines offers a **Fare Hold** option on selected routes:

| Fare Class | Hold Period | Hold Fee |
|---|---|---|
| Economy Flex | 24 hours | USD 15 |
| Business | 48 hours | USD 30 |
| First | 72 hours | USD 50 |

The hold fee is non-refundable. If payment is not completed within the hold period, the booking is automatically cancelled and the fare is released. Economy Saver fares cannot be held.

---

## 8. Multi-City Bookings

Multi-city itineraries (more than two separate flight segments) are available via the Meridian Airlines website under the "Multi-City" booking tab. Each segment is priced independently based on its applicable fare class. Multi-city bookings involving three or more carriers require contact with the Meridian Contact Centre at +1-800-MERIDIAN.

# Meridian Airlines — Booking Modifications Policy
**Policy Reference:** MA-BK-002
**Effective Date:** 1 November 2025
**Applies To:** All fare classes, all routes

---

## 1. What Can Be Modified

The following elements of a confirmed Meridian Airlines booking may be modified subject to fare rules and availability:

- **Travel date** (same route, different date)
- **Travel time** (same route and date, different flight)
- **Origin or destination** (route change — see Section 4)
- **Cabin class** (upgrade or downgrade)
- **Connecting point** (for multi-segment itineraries)

Passenger name changes are handled under a separate policy (MA-BK-003).

---

## 2. Modification Fees by Fare Class

### 2.1 Date and Time Changes

| Fare Class | More than 7 Days Before | 2–7 Days Before | Within 48 Hours |
|---|---|---|---|
| Economy Saver | USD 75 per person | USD 100 per person | Not permitted |
| Economy Flex | USD 0 (free) | USD 0 (free) | USD 50 per person |
| Business | USD 0 (free) | USD 0 (free) | USD 0 (free) |
| First | USD 0 (free) | USD 0 (free) | USD 0 (free) |

Fare difference applies in all cases. If the new fare is lower, no refund is issued on Economy Saver. A travel credit is issued for Economy Flex, Business, and First.

### 2.2 Domestic Route Discount
Domestic route modifications carry a 50% reduction on fees listed in Section 2.1 for Economy Saver only.

---

## 3. How to Modify a Booking

Modifications may be made through:

1. **Meridian Website / App:** Log in → Manage Booking → Enter PNR (e.g., MA-XKPL92) and last name → Select "Change Flight"
2. **Meridian Contact Centre:** +1-800-MERIDIAN (service fee of USD 25 applies for agent-assisted changes)
3. **Airport Ticket Office:** Subject to staff availability and departure timing

When modifying online, the new fare at time of modification applies. If the new fare is higher, the fare difference is charged immediately. If lower, a travel credit is issued (Economy Flex and above).

---

## 4. Route Changes

A route change is defined as any modification to the origin, destination, or both.

| Fare Class | Route Change Fee | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Economy Saver | USD 150 per person + fare difference | Subject to availability |
| Economy Flex | USD 75 per person + fare difference | Subject to availability |
| Business | USD 0 + fare difference | Subject to availability |
| First | USD 0 + fare difference | Subject to availability |

Route changes on Economy Saver are not permitted within 48 hours of departure.

---

## 5. Cabin Class Changes

### 5.1 Upgrades
Passengers may upgrade to a higher cabin class at any time before check-in closes, subject to seat availability. The upgrade fee is the fare difference between the original fare and the applicable fare in the new cabin. Meridian Miles may be used to cover the fare difference.

### 5.2 Downgrades
Voluntary downgrades are permitted on Economy Flex, Business, and First. A travel credit equal to the fare difference (not a cash refund) is issued. Downgrades on Economy Saver are not permitted.

---

## 6. Modification Limits

- A maximum of **3 modifications** are permitted per booking on Economy Saver.
- Economy Flex, Business, and First have no limit on the number of modifications.
- Each modification retains the original PNR and ticket number. Example PNR: MA-XKPL92.

---

## 7. Codeshare and Partner Flights

Modifications to flights operated by codeshare or interline partners must be processed through the Meridian Contact Centre at +1-800-MERIDIAN. Online self-service modification is not available for partner-operated segments. Additional partner airline fees may apply.

---

## 8. Group Booking Modifications

For group bookings (10+ passengers), modifications must be submitted in writing to groupbookings@meridianair.com at least 14 days before departure. Individual passenger modifications within a group booking are subject to group contract terms negotiated at time of booking.

# Meridian Airlines — Business & First Class Cancellation Policy
**Policy Reference:** MA-CX-002
**Effective Date:** 1 November 2025
**Applies To:** Business and First class fare classes

---

## 1. Business Class — Cancellation Rules

Business class tickets are **fully refundable** with the following fee structure:

### 1.1 Standard Business Cancellation Fees

| Cancellation Timing | Fee | Refund |
|---|---|---|
| More than 24 hours before departure | USD 0 | Full cash refund to original payment method |
| Within 24 hours of departure | USD 75 per person | Remainder as cash refund |
| After scheduled departure (no-show) | USD 150 per person | Remainder as Meridian travel credit (24 months) |

### 1.2 Meridian Miles Gold and Platinum Waiver
Meridian Miles Gold and Platinum members receive a **full waiver of all cancellation fees** on Business class tickets, including within 24 hours of departure. The waiver applies to the member's ticket only, not to companion tickets on the same booking.

### 1.3 Corporate Account Waiver
Passengers holding a confirmed Meridian corporate account fare (fare code beginning with **MA-CN**) are exempt from all cancellation fees regardless of timing.

---

## 2. First Class — Cancellation Rules

First class tickets carry the most flexible cancellation terms.

### 2.1 First Class Cancellation Fees

| Cancellation Timing | Fee | Refund |
|---|---|---|
| Any time before scheduled departure | USD 0 | Full cash refund to original payment method |
| After scheduled departure (no-show) | USD 0 | Full cash refund to original payment method |

First class is unconditionally refundable. There are no cancellation fees under any circumstances.

### 2.2 First Class Refund Processing
Full cash refunds are processed within:
- Credit/debit card: **5–7 business days**
- Meridian Wallet: **24 hours**
- Bank transfer: **7–10 business days**

---

## 3. How to Cancel

### 3.1 Self-Service (Recommended)
- **Meridian Website / App:** Manage Booking → Enter PNR (e.g., MA-XKPL92) and last name → Select "Cancel Booking"
- Available 24/7, no service fee

### 3.2 Contact Centre
- Phone: +1-800-MERIDIAN (select option 2 for cancellations)
- No service fee for Business and First class cancellations
- Available 24/7

### 3.3 Airport Ticket Office
- Available up to gate close time for same-day departures

---

## 4. Involuntary Cancellation by Meridian Airlines

If Meridian Airlines cancels a Business or First class flight:
- Full cash refund is processed automatically within **72 hours** without requiring passenger action
- Alternative rebooking on the next available Meridian flight is offered at no charge
- If the alternative involves a lower cabin class, the fare difference is refunded immediately
- Hotel accommodation is provided if the delay requires an overnight stay (see MA-DC-002)

---

## 5. Partial Ticket Cancellations

### 5.1 Return Journey (One Leg)
Business and First class passengers may cancel either leg of a return journey independently without affecting the other leg. The remaining leg retains its original fare and conditions.

### 5.2 Multi-Segment Itineraries
Each segment of a multi-segment itinerary can be cancelled independently. The applicable fee is based on the time remaining before that specific segment's departure.

---

## 6. Award Tickets Cancelled in Business/First

Meridian Miles award tickets booked in Business or First class:
- Miles are re-credited to the member's account (e.g., MA-88234410) within **72 hours**
- Award reinstatement fee: USD 50 per ticket (waived for Gold and Platinum members)
- Taxes paid at time of award booking are refunded per standard timelines

# Meridian Airlines — Economy Class Cancellation Policy
**Policy Reference:** MA-CX-001
**Effective Date:** 1 November 2025
**Applies To:** Economy Saver and Economy Flex fare classes

---

## 1. Economy Saver — Cancellation Rules

Economy Saver is a **non-refundable** fare. The following rules apply:

### 1.1 Cancellation Fees

| Cancellation Timing | Fee | Refund |
|---|---|---|
| More than 7 days before departure | USD 100 per person | Remainder as Meridian travel credit |
| 2–7 days before departure | USD 150 per person | Remainder as Meridian travel credit |
| Within 48 hours of departure | USD 200 per person | Remainder as Meridian travel credit |
| After departure (no-show) | Full fare forfeited | No refund, no credit |

Travel credits issued for Economy Saver cancellations:
- Valid for **12 months** from the original ticket issue date
- Applicable to Meridian Airlines flights only
- Non-transferable (must be used by the original passenger)
- Credit code format: **MA-TC-XXXXXX** (e.g., MA-TC-XKP192)

### 1.2 24-Hour Risk-Free Cancellation
Bookings made at least **7 days before departure** may be cancelled within **24 hours of booking** for a full refund to the original payment method, regardless of fare class. This window is available once per booking.

---

## 2. Economy Flex — Cancellation Rules

Economy Flex is a **partially refundable** fare.

### 2.1 Cancellation Fees

| Cancellation Timing | Fee | Refund |
|---|---|---|
| More than 7 days before departure | USD 50 per person | Cash refund to original payment method |
| 2–7 days before departure | USD 75 per person | Cash refund to original payment method |
| Within 48 hours of departure | USD 100 per person | Cash refund to original payment method |
| After departure (no-show) | USD 150 per person | Remainder as Meridian travel credit |

### 2.2 Refund Processing Time
Cash refunds for Economy Flex cancellations are processed within:
- Credit/debit card: **7–10 business days**
- Meridian Wallet: **24–48 hours**
- Bank transfer: **10–14 business days**

---

## 3. How to Cancel

Cancellations can be submitted through:

1. **Meridian Website / App:** Manage Booking → Enter PNR (e.g., MA-XKPL92) and last name → Select "Cancel Booking"
2. **Contact Centre:** +1-800-MERIDIAN (agent fee of USD 25 applies)
3. **Airport Ticket Office:** Prior to check-in closing time only

---

## 4. Partial Cancellations (One Leg of a Return)

For return tickets, passengers may cancel one leg independently:

| Scenario | Economy Saver | Economy Flex |
|---|---|---|
| Cancel outbound only | Permitted, fees apply | Permitted, fees apply |
| Cancel return only | Permitted, fees apply | Permitted, fees apply |
| Cancel both legs | Treated as full cancellation | Treated as full cancellation |

Cancelling one leg does not entitle the passenger to a refund on the remaining leg.

---

## 5. Taxes and Government Fees

Unused government taxes and airport fees are **fully refundable** on all Economy fares regardless of cancellation timing, unless the tax is non-refundable by regulation. Airline-imposed charges (fuel surcharge, carrier surcharge) are not refundable on Economy Saver.

---

## 6. Promotional and Sale Fares

Special promotional fares (marked "PROMO" or "SALE" at time of booking) are **non-cancellable and non-refundable** under any circumstances, including the 24-hour risk-free window. Meridian travel credits are not issued on promotional fares. Passengers should review fare conditions carefully before purchasing.

# Meridian Airlines — Carry-On Baggage Policy
**Policy Reference:** MA-BG-001
**Effective Date:** 1 November 2025
**Applies To:** All fare classes, all routes

---

## 1. Carry-On Allowance by Fare Class

| Fare Class | Cabin Bag | Personal Item | Total |
|---|---|---|---|
| Economy Saver | 1 bag, max 7 kg | 1 item, max 2 kg | 9 kg |
| Economy Flex | 1 bag, max 10 kg | 1 item, max 3 kg | 13 kg |
| Business | 2 bags, max 14 kg total | 1 item, max 3 kg | 17 kg |
| First | 2 bags, max 16 kg total | 1 item, max 3 kg | 19 kg |

---

## 2. Cabin Bag Size Limits

The cabin bag must fit in the overhead bin. Maximum dimensions:

| Measurement | Limit |
|---|---|
| Length | 56 cm (22 inches) |
| Width | 36 cm (14 inches) |
| Depth | 23 cm (9 inches) |
| Combined (L+W+D) | 115 cm (45 inches) |

These dimensions include wheels, handles, and exterior pockets. Bags exceeding these dimensions will be gate-checked at the passenger's expense (USD 35).

---

## 3. Personal Item Dimensions

A personal item must fit under the seat in front. Maximum dimensions:

| Measurement | Limit |
|---|---|
| Length | 40 cm (16 inches) |
| Width | 30 cm (12 inches) |
| Depth | 15 cm (6 inches) |

Accepted personal items: handbag, laptop bag, small backpack, camera bag, duty-free shopping bag (single, soft-sided).

---

## 4. Prohibited Items in Cabin Baggage

- Sharp objects: knives, scissors with blades over 6 cm, box cutters
- Liquids over 100 ml per container
- Aerosols over 100 ml
- Lighters — one disposable lighter permitted in pocket only
- Firearms, replicas, or imitation weapons
- Lithium batteries over 160 Wh
- Spare/loose lithium batteries over 100 Wh must be in carry-on (not checked)

---

## 5. Liquids Rule (3-1-1)

- Each container: maximum **100 ml**
- All containers in: **1 clear resealable plastic bag, max 1 litre**
- **1 bag** per passenger

Exceptions (must be declared at security — no quantity limit):
- Baby formula and breast milk for infants on the Meridian booking
- Essential medications with prescription documentation
- Duty-free liquids purchased post-security in sealed tamper-evident bags

---

## 6. Electronic Devices

- Laptops must be removed from bags and placed in a separate security tray
- Power banks permitted in carry-on only (not checked). Maximum: 100 Wh per unit, 2 units maximum.

---

## 7. Gate-Check Policy

If overhead bin space is unavailable, cabin bags may be gate-checked at no charge and returned at the jetway on arrival. Passengers retain their personal item onboard. Meridian Airlines is not responsible for damage to fragile items in gate-checked bags.

Passengers who voluntarily gate-check an oversized cabin bag are charged USD 35.

---

## 8. Infant Carry-On

Infants on a lap ticket are permitted one small bag (max 5 kg) for infant essentials at no additional charge, in addition to the travelling adult's allowance. This bag must fit under the seat.

# Meridian Airlines — Checked Baggage Policy
**Policy Reference:** MA-BG-002
**Effective Date:** 1 November 2025
**Applies To:** All fare classes, all routes

---

## 1. Free Checked Baggage Allowance

### 1.1 Domestic Routes

| Fare Class | Number of Bags | Max Weight per Bag | Max Dimensions |
|---|---|---|---|
| Economy Saver | 0 (no free bag) | — | — |
| Economy Flex | 1 bag | 23 kg | 158 cm combined |
| Business | 2 bags | 32 kg each | 158 cm combined |
| First | 3 bags | 32 kg each | 158 cm combined |

### 1.2 International Short-Haul (under 5 hours)

| Fare Class | Number of Bags | Max Weight per Bag | Max Dimensions |
|---|---|---|---|
| Economy Saver | 1 bag | 23 kg | 158 cm combined |
| Economy Flex | 1 bag | 23 kg | 158 cm combined |
| Business | 2 bags | 32 kg each | 158 cm combined |
| First | 3 bags | 32 kg each | 158 cm combined |

### 1.3 International Long-Haul (5+ hours)

| Fare Class | Number of Bags | Max Weight per Bag | Max Dimensions |
|---|---|---|---|
| Economy Saver | 1 bag | 23 kg | 158 cm combined |
| Economy Flex | 2 bags | 23 kg each | 158 cm combined |
| Business | 2 bags | 32 kg each | 158 cm combined |
| First | 3 bags | 32 kg each | 158 cm combined |

---

## 2. Oversize Bags

A bag is **oversize** if combined dimensions exceed 158 cm but are within 292 cm.

| Route Type | Oversize Fee |
|---|---|
| Domestic | USD 75 per bag |
| International Short-Haul | USD 100 per bag |
| International Long-Haul | USD 150 per bag |

Bags exceeding 292 cm combined must be shipped as cargo.

---

## 3. Overweight Bags

| Weight Band | Domestic Fee | International Fee |
|---|---|---|
| 24–32 kg | USD 50 per bag | USD 75 per bag |
| 33–45 kg | USD 100 per bag | USD 150 per bag |
| Over 45 kg | Not accepted | Not accepted |

---

## 4. Meridian Miles Tier Baggage Benefits

| Tier | Additional Benefit |
|---|---|
| Silver | +1 bag on all routes; weight limit increased to 28 kg per bag |
| Gold | +2 bags on all routes; weight limit increased to 32 kg per bag |
| Platinum | +3 bags on all routes; 32 kg per bag; oversize fee waived |

Benefits apply to the status member's ticket only. Quote your Meridian Miles number (e.g., MA-88234410) at check-in.

---

## 5. Connecting Flights

For itineraries on a single Meridian PNR (e.g., MA-XKPL92), checked baggage is transferred automatically to the final destination. The baggage allowance of the **first departing flight's fare class** applies throughout.

---

## 6. Delayed, Damaged, and Lost Baggage

- **Delayed:** File a Property Irregularity Report (PIR) immediately at the Meridian baggage desk. Reference number format: **MA-DXB-99999** (airline code + 3-letter airport + 5 digits).
- **Damaged:** Report at the Meridian baggage desk before leaving the airport.
- **Lost:** Declared lost after 21 days. Compensation up to USD 1,700 per passenger per IATA convention.

Contact: baggageservices@meridianair.com | +1-800-MERIDIAN option 5.

# Meridian Airlines — Child Fare Policy
**Policy Reference:** MA-FA-002
**Effective Date:** 1 November 2025
**Applies To:** Children aged 2–11 years

---

## 1. Child Age Definition

A **child** on Meridian Airlines is a passenger aged **2 to 11 years** on the date of the outbound departure. Children must hold their own seat and ticket.

- Age 2–11: Child fare applies
- Age 12 and above: Adult fare applies (young persons receive no fare discount)
- Under 2: Infant fare applies (see MA-FA-001)

---

## 2. Child Fare Discounts

Child fares are discounted from the applicable adult fare for the same routing and fare class:

| Route Type | Child Discount |
|---|---|
| Domestic | 25% off the applicable adult fare |
| International Short-Haul | 25% off the applicable adult fare |
| International Long-Haul | 33% off the applicable adult fare |

Child discounts apply to the **base fare only**. Taxes, airport fees, and surcharges are charged at the full adult rate (as mandated by most governments and airports).

Child discounts are applied automatically when the passenger's date of birth is entered at booking. Proof of age (passport or birth certificate) may be requested at check-in.

---

## 3. Child Ticket — What's Included

Children's tickets include the same cabin entitlements as the adult fare class booked:
- Same checked baggage allowance as accompanying adult
- Same seat entitlements
- Same meal entitlements

### 3.1 Child Meals
A child meal (CHML) is available as a special meal request for children aged 2–12 years at no additional charge. Must be ordered at least 24 hours before departure (see MA-SS-004).

---

## 4. Travel Requirements for Children

### 4.1 Accompanied Children (Under 12)
Children under 12 must travel with an accompanying adult (aged 18 or over) on the same booking, unless enrolled in the Unaccompanied Minor (UM) service (MA-SS-002).

### 4.2 Young Persons (12–17)
Passengers aged 12–17 may travel independently as adults. Optional UM service available for ages 12–15 (see MA-SS-002).

---

## 5. Child ID Requirements

| Route Type | Acceptable ID for Children |
|---|---|
| Domestic | Birth certificate or national ID |
| International | Valid passport (most countries do not permit children on a parent's passport) |

Children's passports must also meet the 6-month validity requirement at the time of return travel (see MA-CK-004).

---

## 6. Booking Child Tickets

Child tickets can be booked through all standard channels:
- Meridian website (select passenger type "Child" and enter date of birth)
- Meridian app
- Contact Centre: +1-800-MERIDIAN

**Booking PNR covers all passengers in the same booking** (e.g., 2 adults + 1 child on PNR MA-XKPL92). Each passenger has a separate ticket number (MA-XXXXXXXXXXXXX).

---

## 7. Seat Seating Guarantee for Children

Meridian Airlines guarantees that children under 12 are seated adjacent to at least one accompanying adult **at no extra charge** when booked on the same PNR at least 48 hours before departure. If Meridian cannot seat the child adjacent to an adult due to aircraft change or full flight, Meridian will upgrade the group to available adjacent seats at no charge. See MA-CK-003 for seat selection details.

# Meridian Airlines — Flight Delay Compensation Policy
**Policy Reference:** MA-DC-001
**Effective Date:** 1 November 2025
**Applies To:** All Meridian Airlines-operated flights (prefix MA-)

---

## 1. Scope

This policy applies to delays caused by Meridian Airlines operations, including technical issues, crew availability, and aircraft substitution. It does **not** apply to delays caused by weather, air traffic control (ATC), strikes outside Meridian's control, or other extraordinary circumstances (see MA-DC-004).

---

## 2. Delay Compensation and Entitlements

### 2.1 Meals and Refreshments

| Delay Duration | Entitlement |
|---|---|
| 2 hours or more | Meal voucher: USD 15 per person (redeemable at airport restaurants) |
| 4 hours or more | Meal voucher: USD 30 per person + free phone calls / internet access (30 minutes) |
| 6 hours or more | Meal voucher: USD 50 per person + free lounge access (economy passengers) |

Meal vouchers are issued at the departure gate or check-in area by Meridian ground staff. Voucher format: **MA-MV-XXXXXX** (e.g., MA-MV-XKP192). Vouchers expire at end of day of issue.

### 2.2 Cash Compensation

Cash compensation applies only to delays that are within Meridian's control and where the passenger was not rebooked to an alternative flight arriving within 2 hours of the original scheduled arrival.

| Delay at Destination | Route Type | Cash Compensation |
|---|---|---|
| 3–4 hours | Domestic | USD 100 per person |
| 3–4 hours | International | USD 150 per person |
| 4–6 hours | Domestic | USD 200 per person |
| 4–6 hours | International | USD 300 per person |
| Over 6 hours | Domestic | USD 300 per person |
| Over 6 hours | International | USD 500 per person |

Cash compensation is paid via the original payment method or as a Meridian travel credit (MA-TC-XXXXXX). Passengers may choose their preferred form.

### 2.3 Hotel Accommodation (Overnight Delays)

If a delay requires an overnight stay:
- Hotel accommodation arranged and paid by Meridian Airlines at a 3-star or above property
- Airport transfers provided
- Meal allowance: USD 50 per person per day
- Passengers must request accommodation at the Meridian service desk before leaving the airport

---

## 3. Right to Rebook

Passengers affected by a delay of 3 hours or more at the destination may choose to:
- Accept the delay and receive the entitlements in Section 2, **or**
- Rebook to the next available Meridian flight to the destination at no charge, **or**
- Cancel the booking for a full refund (including non-refundable fares) if the delay is 5 hours or more

Rebooking requests: meridianair.com → Manage Booking → Enter PNR (e.g., MA-XKPL92) → Rebooking Options, or Contact Centre +1-800-MERIDIAN.

---

## 4. Claiming Compensation

Compensation must be claimed within **12 months** of the delayed flight.

1. Submit claim at: meridianair.com/delayclaim
2. Required: PNR (e.g., MA-XKPL92), flight number (e.g., MA-204), travel date, original scheduled and actual departure/arrival times
3. Processing time: 15–20 business days
4. Contact: delayclaims@meridianair.com

---

## 5. Connecting Flights Missed Due to Delay

If a Meridian-caused delay results in a missed connection on the same Meridian PNR:
- Rebooking to the next available Meridian connection at no charge
- If no Meridian connection within 3 hours: partner airline rebooking arranged
- If arrival at final destination delayed by 3+ hours due to missed connection: full compensation under Section 2 applies

# Meridian Airlines — Denied Boarding (Overbooking) Policy
**Policy Reference:** MA-DC-003
**Effective Date:** 1 November 2025
**Applies To:** All Meridian Airlines-operated flights (prefix MA-)

---

## 1. What is Denied Boarding?

Denied boarding occurs when a passenger holds a confirmed booking and valid boarding pass but is unable to board due to the flight being oversold. This is distinct from denial of boarding due to the passenger's own fault (e.g., missing documents, disruptive behaviour).

---

## 2. Voluntary Denied Boarding (Bumping)

Meridian Airlines first seeks **volunteers** at the gate when a flight is oversold. Volunteers who agree to travel on a later flight are offered:

| Delay on Alternative Flight | Compensation Package |
|---|---|
| Under 2 hours | USD 200 Meridian travel credit (MA-TC-XXXXXX) |
| 2–4 hours | USD 350 Meridian travel credit |
| 4–6 hours | USD 500 Meridian travel credit + meal voucher (USD 30) |
| Over 6 hours | USD 700 Meridian travel credit + meal voucher + hotel if overnight |

Volunteers also receive:
- Confirmed seat on the next available Meridian flight at no charge
- Retention of their original booking status and miles earning on the new flight

---

## 3. Involuntary Denied Boarding

If insufficient volunteers come forward, Meridian Airlines may involuntarily deny boarding. Passengers will be selected in the following **last to be involuntarily denied** order (i.e., these passengers are protected first):

1. Unaccompanied minors
2. Passengers with disabilities or medical needs
3. Meridian Miles Platinum members
4. Meridian Miles Gold members
5. First and Business class passengers
6. Passengers connecting to another Meridian flight on the same PNR
7. Passengers with the highest fare paid (most recent booking date as tiebreaker)

---

## 4. Involuntary Denied Boarding Compensation

Passengers involuntarily denied boarding are entitled to:

| Delay to Alternative Arrival | Domestic | International |
|---|---|---|
| Under 2 hours | USD 300 cash or travel credit | USD 400 cash or travel credit |
| 2–4 hours | USD 500 cash or travel credit | USD 700 cash or travel credit |
| Over 4 hours | USD 700 cash or travel credit | USD 1,000 cash or travel credit |

In addition:
- **Full refund option:** If the passenger chooses not to travel, a full refund is provided regardless of fare class
- **Rebooking:** Next available Meridian flight at no charge
- **Meals and accommodation:** Provided if the alternative flight departs the same day (meals) or next day (hotel + meals)

Compensation is paid at the gate by the Meridian gate agent. Cash or credit choice is the passenger's.

---

## 5. Documentation at the Gate

Meridian ground staff will provide the involuntarily denied boarding passenger with:
- Written notice of their rights
- Denied Boarding Certificate (format: **MA-DBR-XXXXXX**, e.g., MA-DBR-XKP192)
- Compensation voucher or cash
- Confirmed alternative booking reference

The Denied Boarding Certificate is required to claim any future benefits related to the incident.

---

## 6. Exclusions

Denied boarding compensation does **not** apply when:
- Boarding is denied due to the passenger arriving after the check-in or gate deadline
- Boarding is denied due to health, safety, or security reasons attributable to the passenger
- The passenger does not hold required travel documents
- The alternative flight arrives within 1 hour of the original scheduled arrival (domestic) or 2 hours (international)

# Meridian Airlines — Group Booking Policy
**Policy Reference:** MA-BK-004
**Effective Date:** 1 November 2025
**Applies To:** Groups of 10 or more passengers on the same itinerary

---

## 1. Group Definition

A group booking applies to **10 or more passengers** travelling together on the same origin, destination, and travel date. Each passenger in the group must be on the same PNR or a linked group PNR issued under a single Meridian group contract (e.g., MA-GRP-2025-0042).

Groups of 9 or fewer passengers are handled as individual bookings under standard fare rules.

---

## 2. How to Request a Group Quote

Group bookings are not available through the Meridian Airlines website or mobile app.

**Submit a Group Request:**
- Email: groupbookings@meridianair.com
- Phone: +1-800-MERIDIAN (select option 4 for Groups)
- Minimum advance notice: **21 days before departure** for domestic; **30 days** for international

Required information in the request:
- Number of passengers (adults, children, infants separately)
- Desired travel dates and flexibility window
- Origin and destination (or full itinerary)
- Contact name, company/organisation name, email, and phone
- Fare class preference

Meridian Airlines will respond with a group quote within **2 business days**.

---

## 3. Group Fares

Group fares are negotiated based on group size, travel dates, route, and advance booking period.

Group fares are typically **5–15% below published Economy Flex fares** for domestic routes and **8–20% below** for international routes. Business class group fares are available for groups of 20 or more.

Group fares are not combinable with promotional fares, Meridian Miles redemption discounts, or corporate account discounts.

---

## 4. Deposit and Payment Schedule

| Stage | Amount Due | Deadline |
|---|---|---|
| Booking confirmation | 20% of total group fare | Within 5 business days of quote acceptance |
| Passenger name submission | Names for all passengers | 21 days before departure (domestic); 30 days (international) |
| Final payment | Remaining 80% balance | 14 days before departure (domestic); 21 days (international) |

Failure to meet payment deadlines results in automatic cancellation of the group booking. The deposit is non-refundable upon cancellation. Group contract reference numbers follow the format: **MA-GRP-YYYY-NNNN** (e.g., MA-GRP-2025-0042).

---

## 5. Complimentary Seats

Meridian Airlines provides complimentary seats on the following scale:

| Group Size | Complimentary Seats |
|---|---|
| 10–24 passengers | 1 complimentary seat |
| 25–49 passengers | 2 complimentary seats |
| 50–99 passengers | 3 complimentary seats |
| 100+ passengers | 1 seat per 25 passengers |

Complimentary seats are in Economy class regardless of the fare class of the rest of the group, unless upgraded at cost.

---

## 6. Name Submission and Changes

- Passenger names must be submitted in the format: **LAST/FIRST MIDDLE** (all capitals)
- Names must be submitted by the deadline in Section 4
- Name changes after submission: USD 50 per change (up to 5% of group size at no charge)
- Complete passenger substitutions after name submission: USD 100 per substitution

---

## 7. Seat Allocation

Group passengers are seated together as a block where aircraft configuration permits. Meridian Airlines does not guarantee adjacent seating for all passengers in groups exceeding 30 people on wide-body aircraft. Specific seat requests should be submitted with the group contract.

---

## 8. Group Cancellations

| Cancellation Timing | Penalty |
|---|---|
| More than 30 days before departure | Loss of deposit (20%) |
| 15–30 days before departure | 50% of total group fare |
| 8–14 days before departure | 75% of total group fare |
| 7 days or fewer before departure | 100% of total group fare (no refund) |

Partial cancellations (reducing group size) are subject to re-pricing at the applicable fare for the remaining group size.

---

## 9. Group Baggage

Group bookings receive the standard baggage allowance per passenger based on the fare class of the group contract. There is no additional group baggage allowance. Excess baggage fees apply individually per passenger at standard rates (see MA-BG-003).

# Meridian Airlines — Infant Travel Policy
**Policy Reference:** MA-FA-001
**Effective Date:** 1 November 2025
**Applies To:** Infants under 2 years of age

---

## 1. Infant Age Definition

An **infant** on Meridian Airlines is a child **under 2 years of age** on the date of travel (outbound departure date for return journeys).

If a child turns 2 during the trip, they must hold a child ticket for the return journey.

---

## 2. Lap Infant (Infant on Adult's Lap)

### 2.1 Fare
Lap infants travel at **10% of the applicable adult fare** (Economy, Business, or First) on international routes. On domestic routes, the lap infant fare is a **flat USD 25 per sector**.

### 2.2 Restrictions
- Maximum **1 lap infant per accompanying adult**
- Lap infants do not receive a seat; they sit on the adult's lap
- Lap infants are not permitted in exit rows
- Maximum **2 lap infants per row** (safety regulation)
- Infants must be held during takeoff, landing, and turbulence (no separate seatbelt; adult's belt around both is not permitted — infant uses the provided loop belt)

### 2.3 Loop Belt
A loop seatbelt extension for lap infants is provided by cabin crew on request. One loop belt per infant.

---

## 3. Infant with Seat

Passengers may purchase a full fare seat for an infant:
- Any fare class available
- Infant must travel in an approved child safety seat (CRS) secured to the aircraft seat
- CRS must be FAA/EASA-approved; approval label must be visible on the seat
- CRS installation: airline allows use in all seats except exit rows

Benefits of purchasing a seat for an infant:
- Safer for the child (especially on turbulent or long flights)
- Infant can sleep in CRS more comfortably
- No need to hold infant during the entire flight

---

## 4. Bassinet (Infant Cot)

A bassinet (sky cot) is a small pull-down cot fixed to the aircraft bulkhead wall, available for infants up to **approximately 9 kg and 76 cm**.

### 4.1 Availability
- Available on wide-body international aircraft only (not narrow-body domestic)
- Limited number per aircraft (typically 4–8 bassinets)

### 4.2 How to Request
- Request at time of booking: Contact Centre +1-800-MERIDIAN or specialassistance@meridianair.com
- Reference your PNR (e.g., MA-XKPL92)
- Bassinet request reference: **MA-BS-XXXXXX** (e.g., MA-BS-XKP192)
- Confirmed on a first-come, first-served basis — **not guaranteed**
- Bulkhead seat assigned with bassinet request; extra legroom but no under-seat storage at bulkhead

### 4.3 Restrictions
- Bassinet not usable during takeoff, landing, or turbulence — infant must be held
- Bassinet is fixed to the wall and cannot be repositioned

---

## 5. Infant Baggage Allowance

| Ticket Type | Baggage Allowance |
|---|---|
| Lap infant (all routes) | 1 checked bag up to 10 kg + 1 pram/pushchair (free) |
| Infant with seat | Same allowance as accompanying adult's fare class |

Breast milk, formula, and baby food: no quantity restriction in carry-on; declare at security. See MA-BG-001.

---

## 6. Infant Travel Documents

Infants require a valid passport for international travel. Many countries do not allow infants on a parent's passport. Verify requirements for the specific destination at meridianair.com/travelinfo.

Domestic travel: national ID or birth certificate accepted as infant identification in most jurisdictions.

# Meridian Airlines — Irregular Operations Policy
**Policy Reference:** MA-DC-002
**Effective Date:** 1 November 2025
**Applies To:** All Meridian Airlines-operated flights (prefix MA-)

---

## 1. Definition of Irregular Operation (IRROP)

An irregular operation occurs when a Meridian Airlines flight is:
- **Cancelled** by Meridian Airlines before or after boarding
- **Diverted** to an unplanned airport
- **Significantly delayed** (3+ hours at destination — see MA-DC-001)
- **Operated with aircraft downgrade** (smaller aircraft causing seat removal)

This policy covers airline-initiated disruptions only. Passenger-initiated cancellations and no-shows are governed by MA-CX-001 through MA-CX-004.

---

## 2. Flight Cancellation — Passenger Rights

If Meridian Airlines cancels a flight, passengers are entitled to **one of the following** (passenger's choice):

### Option A — Full Refund
- Full refund of the ticket to the original payment method
- Processed within **72 hours** — no passenger action required
- Applies to all fare classes including Economy Saver
- Refund reference format: **MA-REF-XXXXXX** (e.g., MA-REF-XKP192)

### Option B — Rebooking at No Charge
- Rebooked to the next available Meridian flight on the same route
- If no Meridian flight within 8 hours: rebooked on a partner airline at Meridian's expense
- If downgraded to a lower cabin: fare difference refunded within 7 business days
- No rebooking fee applies regardless of original fare class

### Option C — Meridian Travel Credit
- Travel credit (MA-TC-XXXXXX) for 120% of the original ticket value
- Valid for 24 months
- Applicable to any Meridian flight

---

## 3. Notification Requirements

Meridian Airlines will notify affected passengers of a cancellation via:
- Email to the address on the booking (MA-XKPL92 confirmation email address)
- SMS to the mobile number on the booking
- Push notification via the Meridian app
- Airport display boards and PA announcements

Notification timeline:
- If cancellation is known more than **14 days** before departure: full notification issued
- If cancellation is known **2–14 days** before departure: alternative flight offered simultaneously
- If cancellation occurs on the day of travel: Meridian service desk at the airport handles rebooking

---

## 4. Diversion

If a Meridian flight is diverted to an unplanned airport:
- Ground transport provided to the original destination at Meridian's expense
- If ground transport is not feasible, a connecting flight is arranged
- Meal and refreshment vouchers provided during the wait
- Hotel accommodation provided if diversion results in overnight stay

---

## 5. Aircraft Downgrade (Seat Removal)

If an aircraft substitution results in fewer seats in a cabin:

| Affected Passenger | Compensation |
|---|---|
| Economy Saver | USD 50 service credit + apology letter |
| Economy Flex | USD 100 service credit |
| Business (downgraded to Economy) | Full fare difference refunded in cash + USD 150 inconvenience credit |
| First (downgraded to Business) | Full fare difference refunded in cash + USD 200 inconvenience credit |

Meridian will attempt to minimise downgrades by offering voluntary cabin downgrades with compensation before applying involuntary downgrades.

---

## 6. Extraordinary Circumstances (Force Majeure)

The following circumstances are classified as extraordinary and do not entitle passengers to cash compensation (Options A and B — refund/rebooking — still apply):

- Severe weather conditions that make flight unsafe
- Air traffic control strikes or restrictions
- Security incidents at the airport
- Government-imposed travel bans or airspace closures
- Civil unrest or acts of terrorism

Meal vouchers and hotel accommodation are still provided during extraordinary circumstance delays exceeding 4 hours, even without cash compensation entitlement.

# Meridian Airlines — Name Correction & Change Policy
**Policy Reference:** MA-BK-003
**Effective Date:** 1 November 2025
**Applies To:** All fare classes, all routes

---

## 1. Overview

Meridian Airlines distinguishes between two types of name changes:

- **Minor Name Correction:** A typographical error of up to 3 characters in the passenger's first or last name.
- **Full Name Transfer:** A complete change of passenger identity on a ticket.

These are handled differently in terms of fees, documentation, and permissibility.

---

## 2. Minor Name Corrections

### 2.1 Definition
A minor correction involves fixing a typographical error of **1 to 3 characters** in the first name, last name, or both — provided the ticket clearly corresponds to the same individual.

Examples of permitted minor corrections:
- JONH → JOHN (1 character transposition)
- SMTH → SMITH (1 missing character)
- ALXANDER → ALEXANDER (1 missing character)

### 2.2 Fees

| Fare Class | Correction Fee | Time Limit |
|---|---|---|
| Economy Saver | USD 50 per person | Up to 24 hours before departure |
| Economy Flex | USD 25 per person | Up to 2 hours before departure |
| Business | USD 0 (free) | Up to 2 hours before departure |
| First | USD 0 (free) | Up to 2 hours before departure |

### 2.3 How to Request
Minor corrections must be submitted via:
- Meridian Contact Centre: +1-800-MERIDIAN
- Airport Ticket Office (if within 24 hours of departure)

Online self-service name corrections are **not** available. A copy of the passenger's travel document is required to verify the correct spelling. Always quote your PNR (e.g., MA-XKPL92) when calling.

---

## 3. Full Name Transfers

### 3.1 Definition
A full name transfer replaces one passenger's identity on a ticket with a different person. This is treated as a cancellation of the original ticket and a new booking.

### 3.2 Permissibility by Fare Class

| Fare Class | Full Transfer Permitted? |
|---|---|
| Economy Saver | Not permitted |
| Economy Flex | Not permitted |
| Business | Permitted (see fees below) |
| First | Permitted (see fees below) |

Full name transfers are not permitted within 72 hours of departure on any fare class.

### 3.3 Fees for Full Transfers (Business and First)

| Transfer Type | Fee |
|---|---|
| Same-family transfer (immediate family member) | USD 100 per ticket |
| Non-family transfer | USD 200 per ticket |

Fare difference between original ticket and current fare applies in addition to the transfer fee.

### 3.4 Required Documentation
- Original passenger's government-issued ID
- New passenger's government-issued ID
- For same-family transfers: proof of relationship (marriage certificate, birth certificate)

---

## 4. Title and Suffix Corrections

Corrections to titles (Mr, Mrs, Dr, Prof) and suffixes (Jr, Sr, III) are permitted free of charge on all fare classes up to 4 hours before departure via the Contact Centre, provided they do not alter the substantive name.

---

## 5. Meridian Miles Name Mismatch

If the name on the ticket does not match the name registered to the Meridian Miles account (e.g., MA-88234410), miles will not be credited. It is the passenger's responsibility to ensure name consistency. Retroactive name correction on the Meridian Miles account to match a historical ticket is permitted once per calendar year at no charge by contacting meridiamiles@meridianair.com.

---

## 6. Legal Name Changes

Passengers who have legally changed their name (marriage, court order) may request a free name correction on any active ticket by presenting:
- The original ticket PNR (e.g., MA-XKPL92)
- Legal name change documentation (marriage certificate, court order)
- Both the old and new government-issued IDs

Legal name change corrections are processed only through the Meridian Contact Centre and take up to 24 hours.

# Meridian Airlines — No-Show Policy
**Policy Reference:** MA-CX-004
**Effective Date:** 1 November 2025
**Applies To:** All fare classes, all routes

---

## 1. Definition of No-Show

A passenger is classified as a **no-show** if they:
- Fail to check in before check-in closes, **or**
- Check in but fail to present at the boarding gate before gate close, **or**
- Cancel the booking after the flight has already departed

The no-show policy applies only to **passenger-initiated** failure to travel, not to airline-initiated cancellations or delays.

---

## 2. No-Show Penalties by Fare Class

| Fare Class | No-Show Penalty | Remaining Value |
|---|---|---|
| Economy Saver | Full fare forfeited | No refund, no credit |
| Economy Flex | USD 150 per person | Remainder as Meridian travel credit (MA-TC-XXXXXX, valid 12 months) |
| Business | USD 150 per person | Remainder as cash refund |
| First | USD 0 | Full cash refund |

---

## 3. Return Ticket — Effect of No-Show on Outbound

If a passenger is a no-show on the outbound leg of a return ticket, Meridian Airlines **automatically cancels the return leg** unless the passenger contacts the Contact Centre before the return departure.

| Action | Deadline | Contact |
|---|---|---|
| Notify Meridian of intent to travel on return | Before return flight check-in opens | +1-800-MERIDIAN or meridianair.com |
| Reinstate return leg | Subject to availability + reinstatement fee | Contact Centre only |

**Reinstatement Fees (Return Leg After No-Show on Outbound):**

| Fare Class | Reinstatement Fee |
|---|---|
| Economy Saver | USD 100 + fare difference |
| Economy Flex | USD 75 + fare difference |
| Business | USD 50 + fare difference |
| First | USD 0 |

---

## 4. Missed Connections Due to Passenger Fault

If a passenger misses a connecting Meridian flight due to their own fault (not an airline delay):
- The missed segment and all subsequent segments are treated as no-shows
- Economy Saver: no refund, no rebooking at original fare
- Economy Flex: USD 75 rebooking fee + fare difference for next available flight
- Business / First: rebooking at no charge on next available flight in same cabin

---

## 5. Exceptional Circumstance Waivers

Meridian Airlines may waive the no-show penalty in the following documented cases:
- Medical emergency preventing travel (hospital admission letter required)
- Death of an immediate family member (death certificate + relationship proof)
- Government-mandated travel restriction (official documentation required)

Waiver requests must be submitted within **14 days** of the missed flight to noshow.waiver@meridianair.com. Reference your PNR (e.g., MA-XKPL92) in all correspondence. Waivers are assessed individually and Meridian Airlines' decision is final.

---

## 6. Meridian Miles — No-Show Impact

- No Meridian Miles are earned on no-show tickets
- Elite status members (Gold/Platinum) receive no special exemption from no-show penalties but may apply for exceptional waivers under Section 5

# Meridian Airlines — Refund Processing Policy
**Policy Reference:** MA-CX-003
**Effective Date:** 1 November 2025
**Applies To:** All fare classes, all routes

---

## 1. Refund Eligibility Summary

| Fare Class | Cash Refund | Travel Credit |
|---|---|---|
| Economy Saver | Not eligible | Eligible (post-fee remainder) |
| Economy Flex | Eligible (post-fee remainder) | Eligible (alternative) |
| Business | Eligible (post-fee remainder) | Eligible (alternative) |
| First | Fully eligible (no fees) | N/A |
| Promotional / Sale | Not eligible | Not eligible |

---

## 2. Refund Processing Timelines

| Payment Method | Processing Time |
|---|---|
| Visa / Mastercard | 7–10 business days |
| American Express | 5–7 business days |
| Meridian Wallet | 24–48 hours |
| Bank Transfer (corporate) | 10–14 business days |
| Cash (Airport Ticket Office) | Same-day cash or 10–14 days by cheque |
| Meridian Miles Redemption | 48–72 hours (miles re-credited to MA-XXXXXXXX account) |

Processing times are measured from the date Meridian Airlines confirms the cancellation.

---

## 3. Partial Refunds

### 3.1 Calculating the Partial Refund Amount

```
Refund Amount = Ticket Base Fare
              - Applicable Cancellation Fee
              + Refundable Taxes and Government Fees
              - Non-Refundable Surcharges (Economy Saver only)
```

### 3.2 Tax Refunds
Government-imposed taxes and airport fees are refundable on all fare classes, even if the base fare is non-refundable. Meridian Airlines refunds these automatically as part of the cancellation process.

---

## 4. Meridian Travel Credits

### 4.1 Issuance
Travel credits are issued via email within **24 hours** of cancellation confirmation. Credit code format: **MA-TC-XXXXXX** (e.g., MA-TC-XKP192).

### 4.2 Validity

| Fare Class | Credit Validity |
|---|---|
| Economy Saver | 12 months from original ticket issue date |
| Economy Flex | 18 months from original ticket issue date |
| Business | 24 months from original ticket issue date |
| First | 24 months from original ticket issue date |

### 4.3 Redemption Rules
- Applicable to base fare only, not to taxes and fees
- Non-transferable — must be used by the original passenger
- Can be split across multiple bookings (partial redemption permitted)
- Cannot be exchanged for cash or Meridian Miles
- Applicable only on Meridian Airlines-operated and marketed flights

---

## 5. Refund for Airline-Initiated Disruptions

When Meridian Airlines cancels or significantly delays a flight (see MA-DC-001):
- Full refund to original payment method within **72 hours** automatically
- No passenger action required
- Passengers who prefer rebooking must opt in via Manage Booking or Contact Centre

---

## 6. How to Check Refund Status

- **Meridian Website:** My Account → Refunds & Credits → Enter PNR (e.g., MA-XKPL92) or credit code (e.g., MA-TC-XKP192)
- **Contact Centre:** +1-800-MERIDIAN (have PNR and last name ready)
- Email updates sent at each processing stage

---

## 7. Non-Refundable Components

The following are **never refundable** regardless of fare class:
- Booking fees charged by third-party agents
- Credit card processing fees charged by the card issuer
- Service fees paid for agent-assisted bookings
- Pre-purchased seat selection fees (unless Meridian cancels the flight)

# Meridian Airlines — Special Items & Sports Equipment Policy
**Policy Reference:** MA-BG-004
**Effective Date:** 1 November 2025
**Applies To:** All fare classes, all routes

---

## 1. Sports Equipment

Sports equipment is accepted as checked baggage subject to the fees and conditions below. Each item counts as one piece toward your checked baggage allowance or is charged as an extra bag.

| Sport Item | Max Weight | Max Dimensions | Fee (Domestic) | Fee (International) |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Bicycle (in hard case) | 30 kg | 292 cm combined | USD 75 | USD 100 |
| Golf bag + clubs | 25 kg | 292 cm combined | USD 50 | USD 75 |
| Surfboard / bodyboard | 25 kg | 292 cm combined | USD 75 | USD 100 |
| Skis / snowboard (in bag) | 25 kg | 292 cm combined | USD 50 | USD 75 |
| Kayak / canoe | Not accepted as baggage — cargo only | — | — | — |
| Dive equipment (tanks empty) | 25 kg | 158 cm combined | Standard bag fee | Standard bag fee |

Advance notice is required for bicycles and surfboards: notify Meridian at least **48 hours before departure** by calling +1-800-MERIDIAN or emailing specialbaggage@meridianair.com.

---

## 2. Musical Instruments

### 2.1 Small Instruments in Cabin
Instruments that fit within cabin bag dimensions (56 × 36 × 23 cm, max 7 kg) may be carried in the overhead bin and count as the cabin baggage allowance.

### 2.2 Large Instruments — Seat Purchase
Cellos, large guitars, and similar instruments that exceed cabin bag dimensions but fit within a passenger seat may be transported in the cabin by **purchasing an adjacent seat**. The instrument must be secured in a hard case and fastened with the seat belt. Seat purchase at standard Economy fare; instrument travels at no additional baggage fee.

### 2.3 Large Instruments — Checked Hold
Instruments too large for the cabin and not requiring a purchased seat are transported in the hold:

| Instrument Size | Fee (Domestic) | Fee (International) |
|---|---|---|
| Fits within 158 cm combined | Standard checked bag fee | Standard checked bag fee |
| 158–292 cm combined | USD 75 | USD 100 |

Meridian Airlines accepts no liability for damage to instruments transported in the hold beyond standard baggage liability (USD 1,700).

---

## 3. Mobility Aids and Wheelchairs

Manual and electric wheelchairs are transported **free of charge** on all Meridian Airlines flights, in addition to the standard baggage allowance.

- Manual wheelchairs: accepted in hold or cabin (folding wheelchairs may be stored in overhead bin or closet)
- Electric wheelchairs (lithium battery): battery must be disconnected; maximum battery capacity 300 Wh. Advance notification required at least **48 hours before departure**.
- Mobility scooters: accepted in hold only, same battery requirements as electric wheelchairs

Contact: specialassistance@meridianair.com or +1-800-MERIDIAN option 3.

---

## 4. Prams and Pushchairs

One pram or pushchair per infant ticket is accepted **free of charge** in the hold, in addition to the standard baggage allowance. Prams may also be gate-checked and collected at the aircraft door at the destination.

---

## 5. Pets in the Hold (Checked Cargo)

Pets travelling in the pressurised cargo hold must be booked through Meridian Cargo, not as standard checked baggage.
- Advance booking: minimum **72 hours** before departure
- Pet booking reference format: **MA-PET-XXXXXXX**
- Approved IATA-compliant container required
- Temperature restrictions apply — some routes and seasons have embargo periods

Contact: pettravel@meridianair.com for route-specific availability and fees.

---

## 6. Prohibited Items in Checked Baggage

The following must **never** be placed in checked baggage:
- Lithium batteries (spare/loose) — must travel in carry-on
- Electronic cigarettes and vaping devices — must travel in carry-on
- Flammable liquids (perfume over 500 ml, nail varnish remover over 500 ml)
- Cash, jewellery, passports, or irreplaceable items (Meridian accepts no liability)
- Firearms and ammunition without prior Dangerous Goods approval (MA-HS-001)

# Meridian Airlines — Ticket Validity Policy
**Policy Reference:** MA-FK-002
**Effective Date:** 1 November 2025
**Applies To:** All fare classes, all routes

---

## 1. Ticket Validity Periods

All Meridian Airlines tickets (e-ticket number format: MA-XXXXXXXXXXXXX) are valid for the period stated at time of purchase. The validity period is measured from the **first date of travel** on the ticket.

| Fare Class | Ticket Validity |
|---|---|
| Economy Saver | 12 months from first travel date |
| Economy Flex | 12 months from first travel date |
| Business | 12 months from first travel date |
| First | 12 months from first travel date |

If a ticket is not used and not cancelled before expiry, it is forfeited with no refund or credit.

---

## 2. Minimum Stay Requirements

Certain Economy Saver fares on specific routes carry minimum stay requirements:

- **International routes with "MIN3" condition:** The return travel date must be at least 3 days after the outbound departure date.
- **International routes with "SATNIGHT" condition:** The return travel must include a Saturday night at the destination.

Minimum stay conditions are shown at the time of fare selection and in the booking confirmation. Economy Flex, Business, and First class tickets have no minimum stay requirements.

---

## 3. Open-Jaw Tickets

An open-jaw ticket allows the passenger to fly into one city and depart from a different city.

| Type | Description | Eligible Fare Classes |
|---|---|---|
| Single open-jaw | Fly A→B, return C→A (return from different city) | Economy Flex, Business, First |
| Double open-jaw | Fly A→B, return C→D (different cities at both ends) | Business, First only |

Open-jaw tickets are not permitted on Economy Saver fares. Each sector of an open-jaw ticket is priced independently based on the applicable one-way fare.

---

## 4. Stopovers

A stopover is defined as a deliberate break in journey exceeding 24 hours at an intermediate point.

| Fare Class | Stopover Permitted | Max Stopovers | Fee per Stopover |
|---|---|---|---|
| Economy Saver | No | — | — |
| Economy Flex | No | — | — |
| Business | Yes | 2 | USD 0 |
| First | Yes | 2 | USD 0 |

Stopovers must be declared at time of booking. Adding a stopover after ticketing requires a fare recalculation.

---

## 5. Re-routing

Re-routing is a change to the itinerary that results in a different intermediate point or a different overall routing between origin and destination.

- Economy Saver: Not permitted
- Economy Flex: Permitted with USD 75 fee + fare difference
- Business / First: Permitted with USD 0 fee + fare difference (if any)

Re-routing requests must be made before the first sector departs. Re-routing after travel has begun is not permitted.

---

## 6. Ticket Extensions

If a passenger is unable to travel due to illness or injury, the ticket validity period may be extended upon presentation of a medical certificate:

- Extension period: Up to **3 months** beyond original expiry
- One extension per ticket
- Available on all fare classes
- Request must be submitted to ticketservices@meridianair.com before the ticket expires

---

## 7. Lost or Stolen Tickets

Meridian Airlines issues electronic tickets only (e-ticket format: MA-XXXXXXXXXXXXX). Electronic tickets cannot be lost or stolen. Passengers who cannot locate their booking confirmation should contact +1-800-MERIDIAN with their name and travel date to retrieve their PNR (e.g., MA-XKPL92).

# Meridian Airlines — Upgrade Policy
**Policy Reference:** MA-FK-003
**Effective Date:** 1 November 2025
**Applies To:** All fare classes, all routes

---

## 1. Types of Upgrades

Meridian Airlines offers three upgrade mechanisms:

| Type | How | Who |
|---|---|---|
| Paid Upgrade | Pay fare difference in cash | All passengers |
| Bid Upgrade | Submit a bid; Meridian accepts if seat available | All passengers |
| Miles Upgrade | Redeem Meridian Miles | Meridian Miles members (all tiers) |

---

## 2. Paid Upgrades

### 2.1 Eligibility
Paid upgrades are available on all fare classes subject to seat availability in the higher cabin.

### 2.2 Upgrade Path and Fees

| From | To | Typical Fee Range (per person per sector) |
|---|---|---|
| Economy Saver | Economy Flex | USD 75–150 |
| Economy Saver | Business | USD 400–1,200 (route-dependent) |
| Economy Saver | First | USD 800–2,500 (route-dependent) |
| Economy Flex | Business | USD 300–1,000 (route-dependent) |
| Economy Flex | First | USD 700–2,200 (route-dependent) |
| Business | First | USD 300–800 (route-dependent) |

Exact upgrade prices are shown in real time via Manage Booking → Upgrade. Fees reflect the fare difference between the original ticket and the current lowest available fare in the higher cabin.

### 2.3 How to Purchase
- **Online:** meridianair.com → Manage Booking → Enter PNR (e.g., MA-XKPL92) → Upgrade
- **Contact Centre:** +1-800-MERIDIAN (USD 25 service fee applies)
- **Airport:** At check-in or upgrade desk (subject to availability)

---

## 3. Bid Upgrades (Meridian Elevate)

### 3.1 How It Works
Meridian Elevate allows Economy passengers to submit a monetary bid for an upgrade. Bids are evaluated 48 hours before departure and accepted on a highest-bid basis.

### 3.2 Bid Process
1. Receive Meridian Elevate invitation email (sent 7 days before departure to eligible bookings)
2. Submit bid amount within the minimum/maximum range shown
3. Receive decision via email 48–24 hours before departure
4. If accepted: credit card is charged automatically; boarding pass is reissued

### 3.3 Bid Ranges (approximate, route-dependent)

| Route Type | Economy → Business Bid Range |
|---|---|
| Domestic | USD 50 – USD 250 |
| International Short-Haul | USD 100 – USD 500 |
| International Long-Haul | USD 200 – USD 1,000 |

Declined bids are not charged. Only one bid per booking per sector.

---

## 4. Miles Upgrades

### 4.1 Eligibility
Meridian Miles members (all tiers) may redeem miles for upgrades subject to upgrade seat availability (different from standard seat availability).

### 4.2 Miles Required (per person, one way)

| From | To | Domestic | Intl Short-Haul | Intl Long-Haul |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Economy (any) | Business | 15,000 miles | 25,000 miles | 45,000 miles |
| Economy (any) | First | 25,000 miles | 40,000 miles | 70,000 miles |
| Business | First | 15,000 miles | 20,000 miles | 35,000 miles |

### 4.3 Co-payment
Miles upgrades require a co-payment of government taxes and airport fees for the higher cabin. The co-payment is charged to the card on file.

### 4.4 How to Request
- **Online:** meridianair.com → My Miles → Upgrade with Miles → Enter PNR
- **Contact Centre:** +1-800-MERIDIAN (no service fee for miles upgrades)
- Miles upgrades may be requested up to **2 hours before departure** subject to availability.

---

## 5. Upgrade Waitlist

If the desired upgrade cabin is full, passengers may join the upgrade waitlist:
- Waitlist priority order: Platinum → Gold → Silver → Blue (base tier)
- Within the same tier: higher miles balance takes priority
- Waitlist confirmation sent via email/SMS when upgrade clears
- Waitlist reference format: **MA-UWL-XXXXXX** (e.g., MA-UWL-XKP192)

# Meridian Airlines — Waitlist & Standby Travel Policy
**Policy Reference:** MA-FK-004
**Effective Date:** 1 November 2025
**Applies To:** All fare classes, all routes

---

## 1. Flight Waitlist

### 1.1 Joining the Waitlist
When a Meridian Airlines flight is sold out, passengers may join the waitlist for that flight. The waitlist is available for booking on Economy Flex, Business, and First class fares only. Economy Saver fares are **not eligible** for the waitlist.

### 1.2 Waitlist Priority
Passengers on the waitlist are prioritised as follows:
1. Meridian Miles Platinum members
2. Meridian Miles Gold members
3. Meridian Miles Silver members
4. Economy Flex, Business, and First class passengers (by booking date — earliest first)

### 1.3 Waitlist Reference
When placed on the waitlist, a Waitlist Reference Number is issued in the format: **MA-WL-XXXXXX** (e.g., MA-WL-XKP192). This reference is separate from the PNR.

### 1.4 Confirmation
- If a seat becomes available, the passenger is automatically confirmed and notified via email and SMS.
- Confirmation is typically received within 48 hours of departure for popular routes.
- If no seat becomes available by 2 hours before departure, the waitlist booking is cancelled and a full refund is issued.

---

## 2. Upgrade Waitlist

For upgrade waitlists, see MA-FK-003 Section 5.

---

## 3. Standby Travel

### 3.1 Definition
Standby travel allows a confirmed passenger to travel on an earlier or later flight than the one booked, subject to seat availability at the time of check-in.

### 3.2 Eligibility

| Fare Class | Standby Eligible | Fee |
|---|---|---|
| Economy Saver | No | — |
| Economy Flex | Yes | USD 50 per sector |
| Business | Yes | USD 0 (free) |
| First | Yes | USD 0 (free) |

### 3.3 How to Request Standby
- At the **airport check-in counter** on the day of travel: inform the agent of the preferred flight
- Standby passengers are boarded after all confirmed passengers
- Standby reference format: **MA-SBY-XXXXXX** (e.g., MA-SBY-XKP192)
- If standby is unsuccessful, the passenger must travel on their original confirmed flight

### 3.4 Same-Day Standby
Same-day standby (earlier or later flight on the same calendar day) is available at the airport only. It cannot be requested in advance online.

---

## 4. Oversold Flights — Volunteer and Involuntary Offloading

### 4.1 Volunteer Bump
When a Meridian Airlines flight is oversold, Meridian will first seek **volunteers** to accept a later flight in exchange for compensation:

| Delay Accepted | Compensation Offered |
|---|---|
| Up to 3 hours | USD 200 Meridian travel credit (MA-TC-XXXXXX) |
| 3–6 hours | USD 400 Meridian travel credit |
| Over 6 hours | USD 600 Meridian travel credit + meal voucher |

Volunteers also retain their original ticket for travel on the later flight.

### 4.2 Involuntary Offloading
If insufficient volunteers come forward, Meridian Airlines may involuntarily offload passengers. Priority for offloading (last to be offloaded):
1. Unaccompanied minors
2. Passengers with medical needs
3. Platinum and Gold status members
4. Business and First class passengers

Passengers involuntarily offloaded are entitled to denied boarding compensation per MA-DC-003.

# Meridian Airlines — Weather & Force Majeure Disruption Policy
**Policy Reference:** MA-DC-004
**Effective Date:** 1 November 2025
**Applies To:** All Meridian Airlines-operated flights (prefix MA-)

---

## 1. Force Majeure Definition

Force majeure events are circumstances beyond Meridian Airlines' reasonable control that make it impossible or unsafe to operate a flight as scheduled. These include:

- **Severe weather:** Hurricanes, blizzards, volcanic ash clouds, dense fog, or lightning conditions preventing safe operation
- **Air Traffic Control (ATC) restrictions:** Government-imposed ground stops, capacity restrictions, or ATC strikes
- **Airport closures:** Runway closures, terminal evacuations, or airport operational failures
- **Security incidents:** Bomb threats, security breaches, or terrorism-related closures
- **Government actions:** Airspace bans, border closures, or military restrictions
- **Natural disasters:** Earthquakes, floods, or other events affecting airport infrastructure

---

## 2. Passenger Rights Under Force Majeure

Under force majeure, Meridian Airlines is **not liable for cash compensation** as defined in MA-DC-001 or MA-DC-003. However, the following passenger rights remain in effect:

### 2.1 Rebooking (Always Applies)
- Meridian will rebook passengers on the next available Meridian flight at no charge
- If no Meridian flight within 12 hours: partner airline rebooking arranged where possible
- If no alternative within 24 hours: full refund must be offered

### 2.2 Care and Assistance (Applies for Delays Over 3 Hours)

| Delay Duration | Entitlement |
|---|---|
| 3–6 hours | Meal voucher USD 20 (MA-MV-XXXXXX) |
| 6–12 hours | Meal voucher USD 40 |
| Over 12 hours | Meal voucher USD 60 + hotel accommodation if required + airport transfer |

Care and assistance entitlements apply during force majeure even though cash compensation does not.

---

## 3. Weather Waiver Policy

When Meridian Airlines proactively identifies severe weather affecting a route, a **Weather Waiver** is issued for affected booking dates. The waiver reference format is: **MA-WW-XXXXXX** (e.g., MA-WW-XKP192).

A Weather Waiver allows affected passengers to:
- Rebook to any Meridian flight on the same route within **14 days** of the original travel date at no change fee
- Cancel for a full refund regardless of fare class
- Rebook to a different destination (subject to fare difference) to avoid the weather-affected region

### 3.1 How to Find Your Weather Waiver
- Meridian website: Travel Alerts → Active Waivers → Enter departure date and route
- Email notification sent to booking address if the booking PNR (e.g., MA-XKPL92) is in the affected pool
- Contact Centre: +1-800-MERIDIAN

---

## 4. Long-Duration Disruptions (Over 5 Hours)

For disruptions expected to last over 5 hours from the scheduled departure time:
- Meridian will provide a written status update to all affected passengers every 2 hours
- Passengers may cancel their journey at any point and receive a full refund, regardless of fare class and original refundability conditions
- Meridian staff will be stationed at affected departure gates

---

## 5. Passenger Responsibilities

During a weather or force majeure disruption, passengers should:
- Monitor the Meridian app and email for updates (opt-in to push notifications in app settings)
- Do not leave the airport without checking with Meridian ground staff if rebooking is needed
- Keep booking PNR (e.g., MA-XKPL92) and e-ticket number (MA-XXXXXXXXXXXXX) accessible for rebooking

Passengers who leave the airport before rebooking is confirmed may lose their place in the rebooking queue.
